question: 路特斯科技的全年销售指引是多少？目前看是否能完成？
rewritten: What is the full-year sales guidance for Lotus Technology? Is it currently on track to meet this guidance?
answer:
Lotus Technology 的全年交付目标是12,000辆，同比增长超过70%。根据目前的业绩表现来看，这一目标实现的可能性较大。截止2024年第三季度末
，公司已经交付了超过7,600辆车，与去年同期相比增长了136%。按照这个趋势，Lotus Technology 有望在第四季度继续保持强劲的增长势头，从而实现
全年交付目标。
content: What is Lotus Technology's future guidance?Lotus Technology's annual delivery target is 12,000 units, with year-over-year growth exceeding 70%. Through the Win26 program, Lotus Technology aims to achieve positive cash flow and profitability by 2026.

New York – November 12, 2024 – Lotus Technology Inc. (“Lotus” or the “Company”), a leading global intelligent and luxury mobility provider, today announced its vehicle delivery results for the first ten months of 2024. Lotus Technology Inc. continues to achieve top-rate growth among traditional luxury car brands, delivering 8,631 vehicles in the first ten months of 2024. Europe and China continued to be the major markets for Lotus Technology, with each region contributing about 35% and 25% of the total deliveries, respectively. In October, Lotus Technology Inc. started an open beta test for its urban NOA (Navigate on Autopilot) in key cities. Certain over-the-air updates of intelligent driving, including highway assistant driving functions, were launched in Europe. As previously announced, Lotus Robotics, Lotus Technology's intelligent driving arm, provides intelligent driving solutions with global coverage to both Lotus-brand intelligent vehicles and other global leading auto conglomerates such as Lynk&Co, Farizon Auto, and a top Japanese Tier-one supplier. In October, Lotus Technology Inc. was awarded the Highly Commends honor at the Reuters Sustainability Awards 2024 in the Business Transformation category as the only awardee from the automotive industry. Note 1: Invoiced deliveries, including commissioned deliveries in the US market.

Over 7,600 vehicles were delivered in the first nine months of 2024, a year-on-year increase of 136%. Total revenue reached $653 million, more than doubling year-on-year. Revenue from the intelligent driving business generated from customers other than Lotus surged to $11 million, with a year-on-year growth of 450%. Lotus Technology launched Hyper Hybrid EV technology that provides high performance with a combined range of over 1,100 kilometers. New York – Nov 21, 2024 – Lotus Technology Inc. (“Lotus Tech” or the “Company”), a leading global intelligent and luxury mobility provider, today announced its unaudited financial results for the third quarter and first nine months ended September 30, 2024.

In the first nine months of 2024, Lotus Technology achieved total deliveries of 7,617 units, representing a 136% year-on-year (YoY) increase. Global allocation was relatively diversified, with each region contributing 18-35% of the total deliveries in the first nine months of 2024, powered by over 200 stores in prime locations worldwide. The European market continued to expand, with a year-on-year increase of 372% in deliveries during the first nine months of 2024. The Rest of the World region achieved a year-on-year growth of 110% as Lotus Technology continued to make steady progress in its expansion into new markets. In the third quarter of 2024, the deliveries of the Emira commenced in South Africa and India, and of the Eletre in Japan, South Korea, and the Philippines. The Eletre Carbon, a high-performance variant tailored for the North American market, was revealed and is expected to commence deliveries in the first half of 2025. Deliveries of bespoke and collection versions of both the Eletre (SUV) and the Emeya (GT, Sedan) also commenced in the third quarter. Lotus continues to drive technological innovation to meet market demand.

In the first nine months of 2024, Lotus delivered over 7,600 vehicles, marking a 136% increase compared to the same period in 2023, when 3,221 vehicles were delivered. The third quarter alone saw 2,744 vehicles delivered, a 54% increase over the 1,782 vehicles delivered in Q3 2023. Total revenue for the first nine months of 2024 amounted to $653 million, representing an impressive 105% year-on-year growth from the $318 million reported in the same period of 2023. In the third quarter of 2024, revenue was $255 million, showing a 36% increase from the $188 million in Q3 2023. The intelligent driving business showed remarkable growth, with revenue from external customers reaching $11 million, reflecting a year-on-year growth of 450%. Despite this, the gross profit margins declined, with Q3 2024 margins at 3%, down from 15% in Q3 2023. For the first nine months of 2024, the gross profit margin was 9%, compared to 11% in the same period of 2023.

Deliveries of lifestyle vehicles saw a remarkable 126% year-on-year increase in the first nine months of 2024, constituting 52% of the total deliveries. Europe, being the home of the Lotus brand, demonstrated significant growth with a 372% year-on-year increase in vehicle deliveries during this period. The Rest of the World (ROW) region achieved a 110% year-on-year increase, as Lotus continued to expand into new markets. Furthermore, the company has surpassed 200 stores worldwide, with per-store sales steadily improving through operational optimization. The delivery breakdown for the first nine months of 2024 was 52% lifestyle vehicles and 48% sportscars. By region, deliveries were distributed as follows: Europe accounted for 35%, China for 25%, North America for 18%, and ROW for 22%. Regarding the store distribution, Europe held 34%, China had 25%, North America had 24%, and ROW had 17%.

In the first nine months of 2024, Lotus delivered 7,617 vehicles, which marks a 136% increase from the 3,221 units delivered during the same period in 2023. Revenue reached $653 million, representing a 105% year-on-year increase compared to $318 million in 2023. The cost of revenues also rose significantly by 109%, totaling $594 million compared to $284 million the previous year. As a result, gross profit was $59 million, up 75% from $34 million in 2023. However, the gross profit margin decreased to 9% from 11% in the previous year. For the third quarter of 2024, 2,744 vehicles were delivered, a 54% increase from 1,782 in the same quarter of 2023. Revenue in Q3 2024 was $255 million, a 36% increase over $188 million in Q3 2023. The cost of revenues for this quarter was $247 million, up 54% compared to $160 million in Q3 2023.

"Lotus Technology continued to deliver strong operational growth, with total deliveries of 7,617 units in the first nine months of 2024, up by 136% year-on-year, driven by the European market," said Mr. Qingfeng Feng, Chief Executive Officer. "Lotus Technology is excited to see significant progress in its intelligent driving business across the world, with revenue from customers other than Lotus surging to \$11 million, reflecting a year-on-year growth of 450%, contributing about 2% of total revenue. Lotus Technology also launched Hyper Hybrid EV technology, in line with evolving market demands. The company remains committed to executing the ‘Win26’ Plan to drive efficiency and competitiveness, delivering sustainable value for its shareholders and investors." "Lotus Technology has made continual improvements and streamlined its operations to drive efficiency, optimize resources, and achieve a reduction in operating expenses for four consecutive quarters," said Mr. Alexious Lee, Chief Financial Officer.

What is the reason for the company's sales forecast adjustment?Based on Lotus Technology's prudent assessment of the global macro market environment and the uncertainty of tariffs, Lotus Technology has adjusted the delivery forecast for 2024 to 12,000 units.

Lotus Technology's ability to execute effective marketing and attract orders Lotus Technology's results of operations depend significantly on the company's ability to execute effective marketing and attract orders from customers. Demand for Lotus Technology's vehicles directly affects sales volume, which in turn contributes to revenue growth and the ability to achieve and maintain profitability. Vehicle orders may depend, in part, on whether prospective customers find it compelling to purchase Lotus Technology's vehicles among competing vehicle models as their first, second, or replacement cars, which in turn depends on, among other factors, prospective customers’ perception of the Lotus Technology brand. Lotus Technology develops battery electric vehicles (BEVs) and technologies through cutting-edge design, research and development, and sustainable choices. Lotus Technology has a dedicated global team across Europe and China to conduct its research and development activities, such as developing electric powertrain architecture (EPA), intelligent driving systems, aerodynamics, and cloud services, among others, supported by a strong portfolio of intellectual properties. As of June 30, 2024, Lotus Technology had 1,631 research and development employees, which accounted for 54.6% of its total number of employees.

Guided by Lotus Technology's R&D leaders, the global research and development team has extensive experience in the automotive and technology industries. As of June 30, 2024, Lotus Technology's R&D team consisted of 1,631 professionals with extensive knowledge in automotive engineering, software, and artificial intelligence, as well as diversified working experiences from leading vehicle manufacturers globally. Such composition ensures solid technology development capabilities, especially in intelligence and digitalization. The diversified cultural and professional background promotes the exchange of ideas from different perspectives and ensures the generation of innovations. Lotus Technology established Lotus Robotics in China, the arm of its in-house intelligent driving competence. Lotus Robotics is committed to developing advanced intelligent driving technologies to foster global industry growth. Lotus Robotics has established itself as a global premier provider of comprehensive intelligent driving solutions, including advanced intelligent driving platform software, cloud toolchains, integrated hardware and software development, and intelligent driving operation solutions. Starting from 2024, Lotus Robotics has been providing end-to-end intelligent driving solutions to several leading automakers, including Lynk&Co and Farizon Auto.

总销售量的22%。
content: New York – November 12, 2024 – Lotus Technology Inc. (“Lotus” or the “Company”), a leading global intelligent and luxury mobility provider, today announced its vehicle delivery results for the first ten months of 2024. Lotus Technology Inc. continues to achieve top-rate growth among traditional luxury car brands, delivering 8,631 vehicles in the first ten months of 2024. Europe and China continued to be the major markets for Lotus Technology, with each region contributing about 35% and 25% of the total deliveries, respectively. In October, Lotus Technology Inc. started an open beta test for its urban NOA (Navigate on Autopilot) in key cities. Certain over-the-air updates of intelligent driving, including highway assistant driving functions, were launched in Europe. As previously announced, Lotus Robotics, Lotus Technology's intelligent driving arm, provides intelligent driving solutions with global coverage to both Lotus-brand intelligent vehicles and other global leading auto conglomerates such as Lynk&Co, Farizon Auto, and a top Japanese Tier-one supplier. In October, Lotus Technology Inc. was awarded the Highly Commends honor at the Reuters Sustainability Awards 2024 in the Business Transformation category as the only awardee from the automotive industry. Note 1: Invoiced deliveries, including commissioned deliveries in the US market.

The vehicle delivery numbers presented above are derived from reports submitted by Lotus Technology Inc.'s business units. These numbers may differ slightly from the vehicle delivery numbers recorded in the financial accounting and reporting systems announced or to be announced by the company.

Why is the sales volume in China low?China is a new market for Lotus. In the first half of this year, China contributed 25% of the brand's deliveries, while historically, China contributed only about 5% of Lotus's sales. In the context of a sharp decline in sales of luxury brands in China during the first half of this year, Lotus still achieved double-digit sales growth in the Chinese market. China is an important market for Lotus. Lotus will continue to enhance brand awareness in China and strengthen services to increase sales. ⚫ The main reason for the low gross margin is the increase in the cost of the Emira sports car due to inflation, as well as seasonal changes in the product sales mix. ⚫ Lotus's Average Selling Price (ASP) remained above $100,000, achieving growth far exceeding the industry and luxury car segment. Lotus has taken a series of measures to respond to market changes (see below).
The following summarizes Lotus Technology's Group revenues by geographic areas (based on the locations of customers):

In the first nine months of 2024, Lotus Technology achieved total deliveries of 7,617 units, representing a 136% year-on-year (YoY) increase. Global allocation was relatively diversified, with each region contributing 18-35% of the total deliveries in the first nine months of 2024, powered by over 200 stores in prime locations worldwide. The European market continued to expand, with a year-on-year increase of 372% in deliveries during the first nine months of 2024. The Rest of the World region achieved a year-on-year growth of 110% as Lotus Technology continued to make steady progress in its expansion into new markets. In the third quarter of 2024, the deliveries of the Emira commenced in South Africa and India, and of the Eletre in Japan, South Korea, and the Philippines. The Eletre Carbon, a high-performance variant tailored for the North American market, was revealed and is expected to commence deliveries in the first half of 2025. Deliveries of bespoke and collection versions of both the Eletre (SUV) and the Emeya (GT, Sedan) also commenced in the third quarter. Lotus continues to drive technological innovation to meet market demand.

"Lotus Technology continued to deliver strong operational growth, with total deliveries of 7,617 units in the first nine months of 2024, up by 136% year-on-year, driven by the European market," said Mr. Qingfeng Feng, Chief Executive Officer. "Lotus Technology is excited to see significant progress in its intelligent driving business across the world, with revenue from customers other than Lotus surging to \$11 million, reflecting a year-on-year growth of 450%, contributing about 2% of total revenue. Lotus Technology also launched Hyper Hybrid EV technology, in line with evolving market demands. The company remains committed to executing the ‘Win26’ Plan to drive efficiency and competitiveness, delivering sustainable value for its shareholders and investors." "Lotus Technology has made continual improvements and streamlined its operations to drive efficiency, optimize resources, and achieve a reduction in operating expenses for four consecutive quarters," said Mr. Alexious Lee, Chief Financial Officer.

Research and development expenses. Lotus Technology's research and development expenses increased by US$22.3 million from US$152.5 million for the six months ended June 30, 2023, to US$174.9 million for the six months ended June 30, 2024, primarily due to the increase in share-based compensation expense of US$20.0 million. Selling and marketing expenses. Lotus Technology's selling and marketing expenses increased by US$86.0 million from US$118.2 million for the six months ended June 30, 2023, to US$204.3 million for the six months ended June 30, 2024, primarily due to (i) the increase in marketing, promotion, and advertising-related expenses for the expansion of global sales of US$37.1 million, (ii) the increase in exhibition expenses, professional service fees, and other miscellaneous selling expenses of US$28.0 million; (iii) the increase in labor costs of US$12.1 million; (iv) the increase in depreciation of US$5.5 million; and (v) the increase in share-based compensation expense of US$3.3 million. General and administrative expenses.

(“Lotus Tech” or the “Company”), a leading global intelligent and luxury mobility provider, today announced its unaudited financial results for the third quarter and first nine months ended September 30, 2024.

Lotus achieved significant operational efficiency in 2024 by reducing operating expenses for four consecutive quarters. This focus on streamlining operations contributed to their robust performance throughout the year. The company also made notable progress in ESG (Environmental, Social, and Governance) achievements. Lotus was highly commended at the Reuters Sustainability Awards 2024, leading the electric vehicle industry within the Reuters Vision 2045 Campaign. Additionally, Lotus won the 2024 EUCCC Sustainable Business Award and the Green Apple Award for its excellent environmental practices. Further underscoring its commitment to sustainability, Lotus presented a sustainable supplier management case during the Shanghai Climate Week 2024. This was part of the Climate Tech in Focus Report, slated for release at COP29.

How was Lotus Technology's performance in the first half of the year? Total revenue for the first half of 2024 was $398 million, representing a year-on-year increase of 206%. Lotus Technology's Average Selling Price (ASP) remained above $100,000, achieving growth far exceeding the industry and luxury car segment. Lotus Technology's gross margin for the first half of this year was 13%, compared to 5% in the same period of 2023.

In the first half of 2024, Lotus Technology achieved total product deliveries of 4,873 units, a 239% year-on-year increase, making it the fastest-growing brand among traditional luxury car brands. Global deliveries were balanced, with the four major markets (Europe, China, North America, and Rest of World) each contributing 20-30% of deliveries.

In the first half of 2024, as Lotus Technology began collaborating with several leading automotive brands by providing intelligent driving solutions and technology development services, technology-related revenue started to expand. With the continued increase in cumulative deliveries of lifestyle vehicles equipped with advanced hardware, the potential for generating paid software subscription revenue continues to grow.

Lotus Technology launched the Colin Chapman Bespoke Service, offering personalized options, designer customization, limited editions, and exclusive customization to meet the growing demands of its customers. The delivery of customized models is expected to further enhance the brand's luxury positioning and benefit Lotus Technology's long-term profit margins.

The strategy focuses on six key fields: Nature Positive, Climate Neutrality, Sustainable Supply Chain, Inclusion and Equality, Community Commitment, and Transparent Governance. Lotus Technology joined the United Nations Global Compact (UNGC) in May 2023 and has set targets aligned with the United Nations Sustainable Development Goals (SDGs) for each field, embedding actionable frameworks into its operations to achieve the global goals. In June 2024, Lotus Technology was recognized by the Global Zero Carbon City Pioneer Awards at the 2024 Shanghai International Carbon Neutrality Expo for its innovation and exceptional commitment to sustainability and responsible business practices. Additionally, Lotus Technology was nominated as a finalist in the business and operations transformation categories at the Reuters Sustainability Awards 2024, demonstrating global recognition of its holistic approach to sustainability.

New York – Oct 17, 2024 – Lotus Technology Inc. (“Lotus” or the “Company”), a leading global intelligent and luxury mobility provider, today announced its preliminary vehicle delivery results for the first nine months of 2024. Lotus Technology Inc. continues to achieve top-rate growth among traditional luxury car brands, delivering 7,543 vehicles in the first nine months of 2024, representing a year-on-year increase of 134%. Global allocation in terms of deliveries remained well-distributed, with Europe, China, North America, and the Rest of the World regions each contributing about 20-35% of the total deliveries. In the third quarter of 2024, the Emeya (GT, Sedan) was delivered to Europe, whilst the Eletre (SUV) reached markets including the GCC, Japan, and Korea. In September, Lotus Technology Inc. revealed the Eletre Carbon, a high-performance variant tailored for the North American market with a starting price of $229,900. The deliveries of the Eletre Carbon are expected to commence in the first half of 2025. Deliveries of bespoke and collection versions of both the Eletre (SUV) and the Emeya (GT, Sedan) also commenced in the third quarter.

As of June 30, 2024, Lotus Technology had 207 stores in its global distribution network, and it plans to continue expanding its retail network. In 2023 and the six months ended June 30, 2024, Lotus Technology delivered a total of 6,970 and 4,873 vehicles, respectively, consisting of lifestyle vehicles and Lotus-brand sports cars that were distributed through Lotus Technology International Limited (LTIL). The growth of the global luxury car segment is expected to outpace that of the overall car market. Within the global battery electric vehicle (BEV) market, the luxury BEV segment is expected to outgrow the mass market BEV segment. Lotus Technology is well positioned to capitalize on the growth momentum of the global luxury BEV segment by leveraging the following strategies.

● Delivery  
Updates: Lotus delivered a total of 8,631 vehicles in the first 10 months of 2024.  
● New Technology  
Launched: Lotus unveiled its "Hyper Hybrid EV technology" at the Guangzhou Auto Show. The 900V Hyper Hybrid EV technology features a Hybrid Electric Drivetrain and dual Hyper Charging technology: Ultra-Fast Plug-In Charging and Ultra-Fast On-The-Drive Charging, both with industry-leading charging speeds. The Hyper Hybrid technology enables high performance, high efficiency, and a flexible longer driving distance of over 1,100 kilometers.  
● Delivery of Urban NOA (Navigate on Autopilot) Service and OTA: Lotus started open beta testing for urban NOA in key cities in October 2024, following the launch of highway OTA services. Certain OTA features, including the highway assistant driving function, have been launched in Europe.  
● Reuters Sustainability Awards 2024: In October, Lotus was awarded the Highly Commended honor at the Reuters Sustainability Awards 2024 in the Business Transformation category as the only awardee from the automotive industry.

question: 路特斯科技的平均MSRP在什么水平？
rewritten: What is the average MSRP for Lotus Technology?
answer: Lotus Technology 的车辆平均售价 (MSRP) 超过 100,000 美元。这一价格区间涵盖了其多款车型，如 Eletre 和 Emira。
content:
How was Lotus Technology's performance in the first half of the year? Total revenue for the first half of 2024 was $398 million, representing a year-on-year increase of 206%. Lotus Technology's Average Selling Price (ASP) remained above $100,000, achieving growth far exceeding the industry and luxury car segment. Lotus Technology's gross margin for the first half of this year was 13%, compared to 5% in the same period of 2023.

In the first half of 2024, Lotus Technology achieved total product deliveries of 4,873 units, a 239% year-on-year increase, making it the fastest-growing brand among traditional luxury car brands. Global deliveries were balanced, with the four major markets (Europe, China, North America, and Rest of World) each contributing 20-30% of deliveries.

In the first half of 2024, as Lotus Technology began collaborating with several leading automotive brands by providing intelligent driving solutions and technology development services, technology-related revenue started to expand. With the continued increase in cumulative deliveries of lifestyle vehicles equipped with advanced hardware, the potential for generating paid software subscription revenue continues to grow.

Lotus Technology launched the Colin Chapman Bespoke Service, offering personalized options, designer customization, limited editions, and exclusive customization to meet the growing demands of its customers. The delivery of customized models is expected to further enhance the brand's luxury positioning and benefit Lotus Technology's long-term profit margins.

Lotus achieved a 136% year-on-year increase in deliveries, maintaining its position as a top-performing luxury car brand among global competitors. The company delivered over 7,600 vehicles in the first nine months of 2024, making it the top seller among British luxury car brands. Four models were in delivery, each with an average Manufacturer’s Suggested Retail Price (MSRP) over $100,000. The current portfolio includes one SUV, one sedan, and two sportscars. Lotus maintained the MSRP above $100,000 throughout the first nine months of 2024, despite faster-than-sector growth and intensified competition. The intelligent driving business achieved a contract value of $130 million. Lotus Robotics, the intelligent driving division, provided solutions to multiple global auto conglomerates, contributing significantly to this growth. Lotus's global expansion included entering over 10 new markets across the Gulf Cooperation Council (GCC) countries, Asia, and Oceania. The company introduced the Eletre Carbon, a high-performance variant tailored for the North American market, and also penetrated the fleet market for environmentally sustainable corporate clients in Europe.

Type 134 is a D-segment battery electric vehicle (BEV) SUV planned for launch in 2025. With an average manufacturer’s suggested retail price (MSRP) exceeding US$70,000, Type 134 will target a broader customer base, including younger demographics. Lotus Technology expects the annual sales volume of Type 134 to be between 70,000 and 80,000 units starting from 2030.

Launched in 2021 with an average MSRP higher than US$85,000, the Emira is built on a new Lotus sports car architecture. The vehicle utilizes pioneering Lotus bonded extruded aluminum chassis technology. The Emira measures 4,412 millimeters in length with a 2,575-millimeter wheelbase. It delivers a power output of 298 kilowatts. The Emira can achieve a top speed of 290 km per hour and accelerate from zero to 100 km per hour in 4.5 seconds, with a maximum torque of 420 Nm. Launched in 2019 with an average MSRP exceeding US$2.2 million, the Evija, the world’s first pure electric British hypercar and a 2020 MUSE Global Design Awards winner, is the first Lotus road car to feature a one-piece ultra-lightweight carbon fiber monocoque chassis. The Evija is 4,459 millimeters long and weighs only 1,887 kilograms. The Evija comes with a 1,500 kilowatt power system, with a WLTP maximum range of 402 km. The Evija can deliver a top speed of 320 km per hour and an acceleration from zero to 300 km per hour in 9.1 seconds, with a maximum of 1,700 Nm of torque.

Type 135 is a battery electric vehicle (BEV) sports car planned for launch in 2026. With an average manufacturer’s suggested retail price (MSRP) exceeding US$95,000, Type 135 will be Lotus's first all-electric sports car, built upon Lotus’s unique sports car platform. Lotus expects the annual sales volume of Type 135 to be between 10,000 and 15,000 units starting from 2030.

Launched in 2021, the Emira is built on a new Lotus sports car architecture. The Emira utilizes the pioneering Lotus bonded extruded aluminum chassis technology. The Emira measures 4,412 millimeters in length with a 2,575-millimeter wheelbase. It comes with a power output of 298 kilowatts. The Emira can deliver a top speed of 290 km per hour and an acceleration from zero to 100 km per hour in 4.5 seconds, with a maximum torque of 420 Nm. With an average MSRP higher than US$85,000, the annual sales volume of the Emira is expected to be between 5,000 to 6,000 units starting from 2024.

Powered by Lotus Technology's high-power dual motor, Emeya can deliver an acceleration from zero to 100 km per hour in 2.8 seconds, making it one of the fastest electric GTs in the world. Equipped with Lotus Technology's supercharging feature, Emeya can reach a range of 150 km with five minutes of charging time and boost up to 80% capacity within 18 minutes of charging time using a 350 kW direct current fast charger. In addition, Emeya has been meticulously designed with the latest sustainable materials to reduce the carbon footprint of its production. Lotus Technology commenced deliveries of Emeya in China in March 2024 and expects to commence deliveries in Europe in the second half of 2024. The average MSRP of Emeya exceeds US$100,000. Lotus Technology expects the annual sales volume of Emeya to be between 30,000 and 40,000 units starting from 2028. Future Models

Lotus Technology offers three different versions of Eletre, namely, Eletre, Eletre S, and Eletre R, to satisfy the various demands of customers. Eletre R, in particular, generates a maximum 905 horsepower (hp) and can accelerate from 0 to 100 km/h in 2.95 seconds. Its 112-kWh battery pack offers a maximum WLTP range of 490 km and can be recharged from 10% to 80% in less than 20 minutes. While offering unrivaled performance, Eletre comes at a better value-for-money proposition — with an average MSRP higher than US$100,000 — compared to traditional luxury original equipment manufacturers (OEMs). Deliveries of Eletre commenced in China and Europe in 2023. Planning is underway for deliveries to the U.S. and the rest of the world. Lotus Technology's second lifestyle production vehicle, Emeya, was launched in September 2023, and deliveries of Emeya began in March 2024. In addition to Eletre and Emeya, Lotus Technology plans to launch two additional fully electric vehicles in the near future, including a D-segment SUV in 2025 and a BEV sports car in 2026.

The Vision80 strategy is dedicated to reviving the Lotus brand globally by focusing on customer satisfaction and shareholder returns. Under this strategy, the Win26 Plan aims for positive operating cash flow and Earnings Before Interest, Taxes, Depreciation, and Amortization (EBITDA) by 2026. Furthermore, Vision80 targets a 4% segment market share in 2028, referring to the luxury car market with an MSRP over $80,000. The graph illustrates the projected growth in Lotus vehicle deliveries. From an average of approximately 1,200 units delivered between 2018 and 2022, the number increased to 6,970 units in 2023. The company forecasts deliveries to reach 12,000 units in 2024, ultimately aiming for 30,000 units by the Win26 target year, accompanied by positive operating cash flow.

Launched in 2019 with an average MSRP exceeding US\$2.2 million, the Evija, the world’s first pure electric British hypercar and a 2020 MUSE Global Design Awards winner, is the first Lotus road car to feature a one-piece ultra-lightweight carbon fiber monocoque chassis. The Evija is 4,459 millimeters long and weighs only 1,887 kilograms. The Evija comes with a 1,500 kilowatt power system, with a WLTP maximum range of 402 km. The Evija can deliver a top speed of 320 km per hour and an acceleration from zero to 300 km per hour in 9.1 seconds, with a maximum of 1,700 Nm of torque. The Evija is equipped with a 93-kilowatt-hour battery pack, with a fast charging time of around 18 minutes to 100% capacity. Lotus began deliveries of the Evija in 2024.

The company expects to capture market share and realize first mover advantages by addressing unfilled demands in the current market. Eletre is a luxury lifestyle E-segment SUV powered by Lotus Technology's 800-volt EPA. Combining its technologically advanced platform with cutting-edge design, Eletre delivers leading performance in acceleration, driving range, and charging speed. Lotus Technology offers three different versions of Eletre, namely, Eletre, Eletre S, and Eletre R, to satisfy the various demands of customers. Eletre R, in particular, generates a maximum of 905 horsepower (hp) and can accelerate from 0 to 100 km/h in 2.95 seconds. Its 112-kWh battery pack offers a maximum WLTP range of 490 km and can be recharged from 10% to 80% in less than 20 minutes. While offering unrivaled performance, Eletre comes at a better value-for-money proposition — with an average MSRP higher than US$100,000 — compared to traditional luxury OEMs. Lotus Technology commenced deliveries of Eletre in 2023. The company's second lifestyle production vehicle, Emeya, was launched in September 2023, and Lotus Technology began deliveries of Emeya in March 2024. In addition to Eletre and Emeya, Lotus Technology plans to introduce two new models in the near future.

question: 路特斯科技在2024年进入了哪些新市场？

answer:
Lotus Technology 在 2024 年进入了多个新市场。具体来说，Eletre 被交付到了海湾合作委员会地区、新西兰、印度和埃及，随后还开始接受澳大
利亚、日本和韩国的生产订单。此外，Emeya 在欧洲开始交付，并在海湾合作委员会地区、日本、韩国和东南亚接受预订。这些市场的扩展进一步推动了 Lotus Tec
hnology 的全球布局。
content: New York – November 12, 2024 – Lotus Technology Inc. (“Lotus” or the “Company”), a leading global intelligent and luxury mobility provider, today announced its vehicle delivery results for the first ten months of 2024. Lotus Technology Inc. continues to achieve top-rate growth among traditional luxury car brands, delivering 8,631 vehicles in the first ten months of 2024. Europe and China continued to be the major markets for Lotus Technology, with each region contributing about 35% and 25% of the total deliveries, respectively. In October, Lotus Technology Inc. started an open beta test for its urban NOA (Navigate on Autopilot) in key cities. Certain over-the-air updates of intelligent driving, including highway assistant driving functions, were launched in Europe. As previously announced, Lotus Robotics, Lotus Technology's intelligent driving arm, provides intelligent driving solutions with global coverage to both Lotus-brand intelligent vehicles and other global leading auto conglomerates such as Lynk&Co, Farizon Auto, and a top Japanese Tier-one supplier. In October, Lotus Technology Inc. was awarded the Highly Commends honor at the Reuters Sustainability Awards 2024 in the Business Transformation category as the only awardee from the automotive industry. Note 1: Invoiced deliveries, including commissioned deliveries in the US market.

In the first nine months of 2024, Lotus Technology achieved total deliveries of 7,617 units, representing a 136% year-on-year (YoY) increase. Global allocation was relatively diversified, with each region contributing 18-35% of the total deliveries in the first nine months of 2024, powered by over 200 stores in prime locations worldwide. The European market continued to expand, with a year-on-year increase of 372% in deliveries during the first nine months of 2024. The Rest of the World region achieved a year-on-year growth of 110% as Lotus Technology continued to make steady progress in its expansion into new markets. In the third quarter of 2024, the deliveries of the Emira commenced in South Africa and India, and of the Eletre in Japan, South Korea, and the Philippines. The Eletre Carbon, a high-performance variant tailored for the North American market, was revealed and is expected to commence deliveries in the first half of 2025. Deliveries of bespoke and collection versions of both the Eletre (SUV) and the Emeya (GT, Sedan) also commenced in the third quarter. Lotus continues to drive technological innovation to meet market demand.

Lotus Technology launched the Eletre in 2022 and commenced deliveries of the Eletre in China and Europe in 2023. Lotus Technology launched the Emeya in September 2023 and began deliveries in China in March 2024, expecting to begin deliveries in Europe in the second half of 2024. Lotus Technology also started deliveries of the Evija in 2024. In addition, Lotus Technology expects to launch new vehicle models in the near future as the company ramps up its business. Automobile manufacturers often experience delays in the design, manufacture, and commercial release of new vehicle models. Lotus Technology plans to target a broader market with its future vehicles, and to the extent the company needs to delay the launch of its vehicles, its growth prospects could be adversely affected as it may fail to grow its market share. Lotus Technology also plans to periodically perform facelifts or refresh existing models, which could also be subject to delays. Furthermore, Lotus Technology relies on third-party suppliers for the provision and development of many of the key components and materials used in its vehicles.

New York – Oct 17, 2024 – Lotus Technology Inc. (“Lotus” or the “Company”), a leading global intelligent and luxury mobility provider, today announced its preliminary vehicle delivery results for the first nine months of 2024. Lotus Technology Inc. continues to achieve top-rate growth among traditional luxury car brands, delivering 7,543 vehicles in the first nine months of 2024, representing a year-on-year increase of 134%. Global allocation in terms of deliveries remained well-distributed, with Europe, China, North America, and the Rest of the World regions each contributing about 20-35% of the total deliveries. In the third quarter of 2024, the Emeya (GT, Sedan) was delivered to Europe, whilst the Eletre (SUV) reached markets including the GCC, Japan, and Korea. In September, Lotus Technology Inc. revealed the Eletre Carbon, a high-performance variant tailored for the North American market with a starting price of $229,900. The deliveries of the Eletre Carbon are expected to commence in the first half of 2025. Deliveries of bespoke and collection versions of both the Eletre (SUV) and the Emeya (GT, Sedan) also commenced in the third quarter.

Lotus Technology has started deliveries of the Eletre to new markets, including the Gulf Cooperation Council region, New Zealand, India, and Egypt, following deliveries in Southeast Asia. Production orders for the Eletre have begun for new markets such as Australia, Japan, and Korea. Lotus Technology also started deliveries of the Emeya in Europe and is taking reservations for the Emeya in the Gulf Cooperation Council region, Japan, Korea, and Southeast Asia. The company managed to maintain an average selling price of over US$100,000 in the first half of 2024, despite faster-than-sector growth and intensified competition. Lotus Technology has assessed evolving market conditions and uncertainties posed by new tariff policies in the U.S. and EU, and the company has revised its delivery target for 2024 to 12,000 units. Lotus Technology launched the “Win26” plan, aiming to achieve positive EBITDA and positive operating cash flow in 2026 by further optimizing its internal processes and structures, implementing overall cost control measures, and recalibrating its product plans to cater to globally diversified markets.

question: 路特斯科技有哪些ESG成就？
rewritten: What are the ESG achievements of Lotus Technology?
answer:
Lotus Technology 在环境、社会和治理（ESG）方面取得了显著成就。首先，所有未来的主流车型都将完全电动化，标志着公司向全电动车阵容的转型。其次，
公司在实现碳中和目标上做出了重大努力，包括在制造工厂采用光伏发电系统，2023年该系统已生成超过9,800兆瓦时的电力。此外，Lotus Technology 
还实施了多种节能减排措施，如干式喷漆室设计、废气焚烧、雨水收集与再利用、废水处理等，以减少环境影响。公司还计划在2038年前实现整个价值链的碳中和。另外，Lot
us Technology 通过教育推广项目和社会支持活动，积极激励下一代参与科学、技术、工程、艺术和数学领域的发展。公司在2023年10月25日发布了首份年度
ESG报告，详细记录了其ESG战略和2022年的成就，并将这些目标与联合国可持续发展目标（SDGs）对齐。
content: Guided by the same principle of the Lotus brand and in collaboration with Lotus UK, the new Environmental, Social and Governance (ESG) Charter formalizes the activities that Lotus Technology has been carrying out. As Lotus Technology transforms rapidly to become a global pioneer of electric performance vehicles, the company recognizes its responsibility to do so in ways that lead the industry in minimizing its impact on the environment, benefiting society and the planet as a whole. ● All electric. The first of Lotus Technology's core Environmental, Social and Governance (ESG) commitments is that, throughout the Lotus product line, all future new mainstream vehicles from Lotus Technology and from Lotus UK will be fully electric. The Eletre, the latest of Lotus Technology's new generation of vehicles, marks the company's transition to a full line-up of electric vehicles. ● Carbon-neutral sustainable development. Lotus Technology plans to achieve its goal of becoming a carbon-neutral company through sustainability in the design of products. Manufacturing sustainability is of high importance to Lotus Technology. Minimizing environmental impacts is one of the most important targets of the manufacturing plant from the very beginning.

To help engage the next generation of Lotus colleagues, Lotus Technology leverages its unique global appeal and stimulates the ambitions of the next generation, especially in the creative science, technology, engineering, art, and mathematics subjects. Lotus Technology helps carry out educational outreach programs and works with communities outside the core business to provide support and stability within those environments. Lotus Technology also adopts and aligns its strategy to external frameworks including the UN Global Compact, including commitments to transparent, responsible, and ethical business management. Lotus Technology released its first annual ESG Report on October 25, 2023, which documented its ESG strategy and highlighted its 2022 ESG achievements as part of Lotus’s advancement into a fully electric, intelligent, and sustainable luxury mobility provider under the brand’s Vision80 strategy. The strategy focuses on six key fields: Nature Positive, Climate Neutrality, Sustainable Supply Chain, Inclusion and Equality, Community Commitment, and Transparent Governance. Lotus Technology joined the United Nations Global Compact (UNGC) in May 2023 and has set targets aligned with the United Nations Sustainable Development Goals (SDGs) for each field, embedding actionable frameworks into its operations to achieve the global goals.

The driving change commitment includes significant reduction in carbon emissions throughout the business, substantial elimination of waste, driving efficient and sustainable use of resources in both operations and supply chains, and protecting and preserving natural environments. Geely Holding’s manufacturing plant adopted a water reuse system that replenishes the water volume of Lotus Lake by recycling roof rainwater. The lake water will be treated and used for greening, flushing, and landscaping. ● Inspiring the next generation. Being at the forefront of electrification and decarbonization, Lotus Group leads the electrification transformation of the luxury car segment and adheres to the highest international ESG standards with an ESG rating of A- by SynTao Green Finance, which awarded A- and above ranking to only 7% of listed companies in its radar. Lotus Group adopts a digital-first, omni-channel sales model that uses digital channels and physical retail services to provide a seamless and unified experience to customers. The Lotus App offers customizable services on the digital platform, providing great flexibility and scalability in response to market and customer requirements. In addition, Lotus Group adopts a direct sales model and has established co-partnership programs with leading automotive dealers across all regions. Lotus Group believes such arrangements can help the company expand quickly while maintaining customer intimacy. Global, experienced, and visionary leadership.

Lotus Technology joined the United Nations Global Compact (UNGC) in May 2023 and has set targets aligned with the United Nations Sustainable Development Goals (SDGs) for each field, embedding actionable frameworks into its operations to achieve the global goals. In June 2024, Lotus Technology was recognized by the Global Zero Carbon City Pioneer Awards at the 2024 Shanghai International Carbon Neutrality Expo for its innovation and exceptional commitment to sustainability and responsible business practices. Additionally, Lotus Technology was nominated as a finalist in the business and operations transformation categories at the Reuters Sustainability Awards 2024, demonstrating global recognition of its holistic approach to sustainability.

Carbon-neutral sustainable development. Lotus Technology plans to achieve its goal of becoming a carbon-neutral company through sustainability in the design of products. Manufacturing sustainability is of high importance to Lotus Technology. Minimizing environmental impacts is one of the most important targets of the manufacturing plant from the very beginning. Lotus Technology has worked with Geely Holding to carry out a series of energy-saving and emission reduction measures at Geely Holding’s manufacturing plant, including adopting the design of a dry box spray room, the establishment of photovoltaic power generation, waste gas incineration, rainwater collection, water reuse, waste heat utilization, and other green facilities. The photovoltaic power generation system at Lotus Global Smart Factory generated more than 9,800 MWh in 2023. The photovoltaic power generation system at Geely Holding’s manufacturing plant has a capacity of over 16 million kWh. Lotus Technology also targets to achieve carbon neutrality (Scope 1, 2, and 3) by 2038. Lotus Technology Inc. has operations across the UK, the EU, and China. The company is dedicated to delivering luxury lifestyle battery electric vehicles, with a focus on world-class research and development in next-generation automobility technologies such as electrification, digitalization, and more. For more information about Lotus Technology Inc., please visit www.group-lotus.com.

Lotus achieved significant operational efficiency in 2024 by reducing operating expenses for four consecutive quarters. This focus on streamlining operations contributed to their robust performance throughout the year. The company also made notable progress in ESG (Environmental, Social, and Governance) achievements. Lotus was highly commended at the Reuters Sustainability Awards 2024, leading the electric vehicle industry within the Reuters Vision 2045 Campaign. Additionally, Lotus won the 2024 EUCCC Sustainable Business Award and the Green Apple Award for its excellent environmental practices. Further underscoring its commitment to sustainability, Lotus presented a sustainable supplier management case during the Shanghai Climate Week 2024. This was part of the Climate Tech in Focus Report, slated for release at COP29.

For parts requiring special designs, Lotus Technology reviews design proposals and chooses largely based on design-related factors. However, in certain cases, Lotus Technology has limited choices given its scale, such as battery cell packages, so in such circumstances, Lotus Technology typically partners with suppliers that it believes to be well-positioned to meet its needs. In addition, when part suppliers are selected, Lotus Technology has established certain environmental guidelines in accordance with its ESG strategies and goals.

question: 路特斯的智能驾驶有哪些成就？
rewritten: What are the achievements of Lotus Technology in smart driving?
answer:
路特斯智能驾驶成就包括：1. 提供L2和L2+级别ADAS/PAS解决方案，覆盖欧洲、亚洲、北美等全球市场；2. 高速公路辅助功能在欧洲升级；3. 建立全球云和
数据中心覆盖；4. 与吉利生态系统、欧洲顶级企业和日本一级供应商合作；5. 未来2-3年为约10款车型提供智能驾驶方案。
content: Since its inception, Team Lotus has won multiple Formula 1 championships, including 6 “FIA Formula 1 Drivers’ World Championships,” 7 “FIA Formula 1 Constructors’ World Championships,” and 81 “FIA Formula 1 Grand Prix Wins.” The Lotus brand was also awarded “Luxury Brand of the Year” at the prestigious “Luxury Briefing Awards 2019,” while the Evija received a “2020 MUSE Global Design Award.” Proprietary next-generation technology built on world-class research and development capabilities. Lotus Group has consistently been a technological pioneer in the automotive industry over the past seven decades. The company's exceptional technologies are demonstrated by its proprietary 800-volt EPA architecture, which entails supercharging capabilities, high energy conservation, and high-speed data transmission, with high adaptability that can accommodate varying battery sizes, motors, and component layouts across vehicle classes. Additionally, Lotus Group possesses leading Advanced Driver Assistance Systems (ADAS) technology with fully embedded Level 4-ready hardware capability, enabled by the world’s first deployable LiDAR system, five 360° perception coverage, and a self-developed software system for cognition, decision-making, design, and control algorithms. The operating system, Lotus Hyper OS, utilizes technology to create real-time 3D content for passengers.

● Intelligent driving hardware. Lotus Technology has invested significant resources into the development of hardware, and L4-ready hardware is embedded in its vehicles. The deployable LiDAR technology is capable of all-around perception coverage with seven HD cameras of eight-megapixel, six long/short-range millimeter-wave radars, among which the front and rear are image radars, four 128-line LiDARs, 12 ultrasonic radars, one in-car camera, and four around-view cameras. By combining radars and LiDARs with camera sensors, Lotus Technology's battery electric vehicle (BEV) lifestyle models can capture speed and other information on the road to support high-level intelligent driving functions. In addition, the Eletre and Emeya models are powered and supported by dual NVIDIA DRIVE Orin chips with 500 to 1,000 TOPS computing power to process complex data and images. The L4-ready hardware on the vehicles is capable of capturing significant amounts of high-quality information, which in turn could be used to optimize key intelligent driving algorithms, thereby helping Lotus Technology remain a leader in the development of such technology. This top-class driving hardware distinguishes Lotus Technology's models from competitors’ vehicles and enables them to evolve with the development of intelligent driving technology throughout their life cycle. ● Software capabilities.

Lotus Technology's intelligent driving research and development team has developed key intelligent driving algorithms, including cognition, decision-making, planning, and control. Intelligent driving-related testing and simulation tools are also being developed to test and train algorithms and build a foundation for cloud-based services. The advanced intelligent driving software and algorithms take into account various scenarios, including highway, urban, and parking. The Eletre and Emeya models come with L2 intelligent driving solutions such as driving assistance, parking assistance, and active safety systems upon delivery. On top of these functions, Lotus Technology is developing end-to-end solutions for scenarios such as highway, urban, and parking, with some solutions integrated into its models as premium functions through over-the-air (OTA) updates. Furthermore, Lotus Technology's intelligent driving arm, Lotus Robotics, has been designated to license several leading automakers to provide them with intelligent driving technology, software, and intelligent driving-related tools. ● Cloud services. Lotus Technology constructed a powerful cloud infrastructure to support cloud-based services for customers. Lotus Technology's cloud services fully empower intelligent driving in data compliance, model training, process optimization, and improve computing power and digital operation capabilities.

Lotus Technology has integrated these hardware, software, and cloud capabilities to develop a full stack of advanced intelligent driving technologies, which enable Lotus vehicles to perceive and intelligently react to their surroundings, thereby enhancing the driving experience. By combining powerful algorithms, HD cameras, radars, and high-precision mapping capabilities, Lotus vehicles are able to precisely perceive their surroundings in stereoscopic display. Lotus vehicles then dynamically react to their perceived surroundings by leveraging customized planning and control algorithms. Taken together, Lotus Technology's intelligent driving solution offers a smooth and intelligent driving experience to drivers, even under extreme road conditions. Lotus Technology has developed an electrical, instrumentation, and control (EIC) system, which enhances the efficiency and performance of battery electric vehicle (BEV) models. With the EIC system, Lotus Technology is pioneering the release of 800-volt architecture and incorporating high-power motors into the e-mobility system. In addition, Lotus Technology's supercharging design features significant charging efficiency to ensure the consistently strong performance of its e-mobility system.

Lotus Intelligent Driving research and development commenced as early as 2018. Lotus Technology is committed to building the best platform for advanced intelligent driving technologies and has strong in-house research and development capabilities, which include cognition, decision-making, planning, and control. Lotus Technology supports end-to-end intelligent driving technologies with expertise in best-in-class hardware, advanced software and algorithms, and powerful cloud solutions. The intelligent driving R&D teams in Germany and China have extensive experience in homologation and a deep understanding of the behaviors and preferences of local customers. ● Intelligent driving hardware. content: Lotus achieved a 136% year-on-year increase in deliveries, maintaining its position as a top-performing luxury car brand among global competitors. The company delivered over 7,600 vehicles in the first nine months of 2024, making it the top seller among British luxury car brands. Four models were in delivery, each with an average Manufacturer’s Suggested Retail Price (MSRP) over $100,000. The current portfolio includes one SUV, one sedan, and two sportscars. Lotus maintained the MSRP above $100,000 throughout the first nine months of 2024, despite faster-than-sector growth and intensified competition. The intelligent driving business achieved a contract value of $130 million. Lotus Robotics, the intelligent driving division, provided solutions to multiple global auto conglomerates, contributing significantly to this growth. Lotus's global expansion included entering over 10 new markets across the Gulf Cooperation Council (GCC) countries, Asia, and Oceania. The company introduced the Eletre Carbon, a high-performance variant tailored for the North American market, and also penetrated the fleet market for environmentally sustainable corporate clients in Europe.

"Lotus Technology continued to deliver strong operational growth, with total deliveries of 7,617 units in the first nine months of 2024, up by 136% year-on-year, driven by the European market," said Mr. Qingfeng Feng, Chief Executive Officer. "Lotus Technology is excited to see significant progress in its intelligent driving business across the world, with revenue from customers other than Lotus surging to \$11 million, reflecting a year-on-year growth of 450%, contributing about 2% of total revenue. Lotus Technology also launched Hyper Hybrid EV technology, in line with evolving market demands. The company remains committed to executing the ‘Win26’ Plan to drive efficiency and competitiveness, delivering sustainable value for its shareholders and investors." "Lotus Technology has made continual improvements and streamlined its operations to drive efficiency, optimize resources, and achieve a reduction in operating expenses for four consecutive quarters," said Mr. Alexious Lee, Chief Financial Officer.

question: 介绍一下路特斯智能驾驶的NOA服务？
rewritten: Can you provide an introduction to Lotus Technology's NOA (Navigate on Autopilot) service?
answer:
Lotus 的 NOA（Navigate on Autopilot）服务是其智能驾驶技术的重要组成部分。2024年10月，Lotus 开始在主要城市进行城市 N
OA 服务的公开测试，这是继高速公路 OTA 服务推出后的又一重要进展。此外，某些 OTA 功能，包括高速公路辅助驾驶功能，已经在欧洲市场推出。Lotus 的 
NOA 服务结合了强大的硬件和先进的软件算法，能够实现全方位感知覆盖，包括七个八百万像素高清摄像头、六个长/短程毫米波雷达（前部和后部为图像雷达）、四个128线
激光雷达、12个超声波雷达、一个车内摄像头和四个环视摄像头。通过这些传感器的协同工作，Lotus 车辆能够精确感知周围环境并智能地做出反应。此外，Lotus 通
过其云基础设施支持 NOA 服务，确保数据合规、模型训练、流程优化，并提升计算能力和数字运营能力。这些技术共同为驾驶员提供流畅且智能的驾驶体验，即使在极端道路条
件下也能保持高性能。
content: New York – November 12, 2024 – Lotus Technology Inc. (“Lotus” or the “Company”), a leading global intelligent and luxury mobility provider, today announced its vehicle delivery results for the first ten months of 2024. Lotus Technology Inc. continues to achieve top-rate growth among traditional luxury car brands, delivering 8,631 vehicles in the first ten months of 2024. Europe and China continued to be the major markets for Lotus Technology, with each region contributing about 35% and 25% of the total deliveries, respectively. In October, Lotus Technology Inc. started an open beta test for its urban NOA (Navigate on Autopilot) in key cities. Certain over-the-air updates of intelligent driving, including highway assistant driving functions, were launched in Europe. As previously announced, Lotus Robotics, Lotus Technology's intelligent driving arm, provides intelligent driving solutions with global coverage to both Lotus-brand intelligent vehicles and other global leading auto conglomerates such as Lynk&Co, Farizon Auto, and a top Japanese Tier-one supplier. In October, Lotus Technology Inc. was awarded the Highly Commends honor at the Reuters Sustainability Awards 2024 in the Business Transformation category as the only awardee from the automotive industry. Note 1: Invoiced deliveries, including commissioned deliveries in the US market.

● Delivery  
Updates: Lotus delivered a total of 8,631 vehicles in the first 10 months of 2024.  
● New Technology  
Launched: Lotus unveiled its "Hyper Hybrid EV technology" at the Guangzhou Auto Show. The 900V Hyper Hybrid EV technology features a Hybrid Electric Drivetrain and dual Hyper Charging technology: Ultra-Fast Plug-In Charging and Ultra-Fast On-The-Drive Charging, both with industry-leading charging speeds. The Hyper Hybrid technology enables high performance, high efficiency, and a flexible longer driving distance of over 1,100 kilometers.  
● Delivery of Urban NOA (Navigate on Autopilot) Service and OTA: Lotus started open beta testing for urban NOA in key cities in October 2024, following the launch of highway OTA services. Certain OTA features, including the highway assistant driving function, have been launched in Europe.  
● Reuters Sustainability Awards 2024: In October, Lotus was awarded the Highly Commended honor at the Reuters Sustainability Awards 2024 in the Business Transformation category as the only awardee from the automotive industry.

In the first ten months of 2024, Lotus delivered a total of 8,631 vehicles, making significant inroads in the luxury electric vehicle market in China. In October, Lotus cars accounted for about 40% of the total luxury electric vehicles (LEV) sold in the Chinese market. The company initiated a beta test for Urban Navigation of Autopilot (NOA) services in key cities in October 2024. This followed the successful launch of highway over-the-air (OTA) services, and included a highway assistant driving function which has already been introduced in Europe. Upcoming events include opening orders for the Emeya GT Sedan in markets such as the Gulf Cooperation Council (GCC), India, Oceania, Japan, South Korea, and Southeast Asia. Lotus Day 2025 is scheduled for March 2025, and Lotus Capital Market Day will occur on December 4, 2024.

question: 介绍一下路特斯的Vision80计划和Win26计划
rewritten: Can you introduce Lotus Technology's Vision80 plan and Win26 plan?
answer:
Lotus Technology 的 Vision80 和 Win26 计划是其长期战略的重要组成部分。Vision80 是 Lotus 为实现品牌转型和可持续
发展而制定的长远计划。该计划的目标是在 2028 年，即公司成立 80 周年之际，将 Lotus 打造成一个全球领先的高性能电动汽车品牌。Vision80 强调
了公司在电动化、智能化和可持续性方面的努力。例如，Lotus 已经推出了 E-segment 的电池电动车（BEV），并计划在未来几年内推出更多 BEV 车型。
此外，Lotus 还致力于通过减少废物、采用可再生能源和可回收材料来实现碳中和目标，预计在 2038 年前达成这一目标。Win26 计划则是 Lotus 在短期
至中期内的具体执行策略。该计划的目标是在 2026 年实现正向的 EBITDA 和正向的运营现金流。为了达到这一目标，Lotus 正在优化内部流程和结构，实施全
面的成本控制措施，并调整产品计划以适应全球多元化市场的需求。Win26 计划还强调了持续的产品创新和技术升级，例如推出 Hyper Hybrid EV 技术和智
能驾驶业务的发展。此外，Lotus 通过提高运营效率、减少运营费用，已经连续四个季度实现了成本的优化。这些计划共同推动 Lotus 在竞争激烈的汽车市场中保持领
先地位，并实现长期可持续发展。
content: The Vision80 strategy is dedicated to reviving the Lotus brand globally by focusing on customer satisfaction and shareholder returns. Under this strategy, the Win26 Plan aims for positive operating cash flow and Earnings Before Interest, Taxes, Depreciation, and Amortization (EBITDA) by 2026. Furthermore, Vision80 targets a 4% segment market share in 2028, referring to the luxury car market with an MSRP over $80,000. The graph illustrates the projected growth in Lotus vehicle deliveries. From an average of approximately 1,200 units delivered between 2018 and 2022, the number increased to 6,970 units in 2023. The company forecasts deliveries to reach 12,000 units in 2024, ultimately aiming for 30,000 units by the Win26 target year, accompanied by positive operating cash flow.

The Lotus WIN26 Plan targets positive operating cash flow and EBITDA in 2026 to enhance profitability and potentially increase returns to investors. Key milestones for 2026 include a vehicle delivery volume of 30,000 units and a gross profit margin exceeding 20%. The plan also aims for positive operating cash flow (OCF) and EBITDA by 2026. A significant increase in vehicle deliveries is projected, with approximately 12,000 units anticipated in 2024 and 30,000 units by 2026, compared to 6,970 units in 2023. The WIN26 Plan is structured around four pillars to drive profitability. The first pillar focuses on revenue by increasing the blended average selling price (ASP) with more limited edition models and enhancing the take rate of high-ASP and bespoke models. The second pillar emphasizes boosting other high-margin business revenues through increased high-margin intellectual property (IP) licensing and paid software subscriptions. The third pillar targets cost of goods sold (COGS) by reducing the bill of materials (BOM) annually and lowering trade-related COGS.

Lotus Technology plans to launch a well-balanced portfolio of new models with exceptional performance in the coming years to broaden its customer base, in particular high net worth individuals and the tech-savvy younger generations, and expand its product breadth. Lotus Technology plans to introduce two new models in the near future. Lotus Technology also intends to upgrade its models to equip its vehicles with innovative technology and design. Focus on sustainability and lead in electrification. As a leader in the electrification transformation of the luxury car segment, Lotus Technology has launched its E-segment battery electric vehicle (BEV) model years ahead of its competitors and plans to continue launching its other BEV models. As part of its sustainability commitment, Lotus Technology aims to achieve carbon neutrality by 2038. Lotus Technology expects to continue focusing on sustainability leadership through waste reduction and the ongoing adoption of renewable energy and recyclable materials. In addition, Lotus Technology launched the “Win26” plan, aiming to achieve positive EBITDA in 2026 by further optimizing its internal processes and structures, implementing overall cost measures, and recalibrating its product plans to cater to globally diversified markets.

To help engage the next generation of Lotus colleagues, Lotus Technology leverages its unique global appeal and stimulates the ambitions of the next generation, especially in the creative science, technology, engineering, art, and mathematics subjects. Lotus Technology helps carry out educational outreach programs and works with communities outside the core business to provide support and stability within those environments. Lotus Technology also adopts and aligns its strategy to external frameworks including the UN Global Compact, including commitments to transparent, responsible, and ethical business management. Lotus Technology released its first annual ESG Report on October 25, 2023, which documented its ESG strategy and highlighted its 2022 ESG achievements as part of Lotus’s advancement into a fully electric, intelligent, and sustainable luxury mobility provider under the brand’s Vision80 strategy. The strategy focuses on six key fields: Nature Positive, Climate Neutrality, Sustainable Supply Chain, Inclusion and Equality, Community Commitment, and Transparent Governance. Lotus Technology joined the United Nations Global Compact (UNGC) in May 2023 and has set targets aligned with the United Nations Sustainable Development Goals (SDGs) for each field, embedding actionable frameworks into its operations to achieve the global goals.

content: In the first nine months of 2024, Lotus Robotics reported a revenue of $19 million, which represents a year-over-year growth of 217%. The revenue from other customers soared to $11 million, marking a 450% increase as the external customer base expanded. Additionally, the revenue from Lotus itself grew to $8 million, reflecting a 100% year-over-year growth. The recognized revenue mix for the first nine months of 2024 comprised 58% from other customers and 42% from Lotus. The contract value distribution by region for the same period revealed that 70% originated from China, while Europe and the rest of the world (ROW) accounted for the remaining 30%.

In the first nine months of 2024, Lotus Technology achieved total deliveries of 7,617 units, representing a 136% year-on-year (YoY) increase. Global allocation was relatively diversified, with each region contributing 18-35% of the total deliveries in the first nine months of 2024, powered by over 200 stores in prime locations worldwide. The European market continued to expand, with a year-on-year increase of 372% in deliveries during the first nine months of 2024. The Rest of the World region achieved a year-on-year growth of 110% as Lotus Technology continued to make steady progress in its expansion into new markets. In the third quarter of 2024, the deliveries of the Emira commenced in South Africa and India, and of the Eletre in Japan, South Korea, and the Philippines. The Eletre Carbon, a high-performance variant tailored for the North American market, was revealed and is expected to commence deliveries in the first half of 2025. Deliveries of bespoke and collection versions of both the Eletre (SUV) and the Emeya (GT, Sedan) also commenced in the third quarter. Lotus continues to drive technological innovation to meet market demand.

question: 哪些市场已经在销售Eletre？

answer:
Eletre 已经在多个市场开始销售，包括东南亚、海湾合作委员会地区（如阿联酋）、新西兰、印度和埃及。此外，Eletre 也在欧洲市场上开始交付，并且已经开始接
受澳大利亚、日本和韩国市场的生产订单。
content: Lotus Technology has started deliveries of the Eletre to new markets, including the Gulf Cooperation Council region, New Zealand, India, and Egypt, following deliveries in Southeast Asia. Production orders for the Eletre have begun for new markets such as Australia, Japan, and Korea. Lotus Technology also started deliveries of the Emeya in Europe and is taking reservations for the Emeya in the Gulf Cooperation Council region, Japan, Korea, and Southeast Asia. The company managed to maintain an average selling price of over US$100,000 in the first half of 2024, despite faster-than-sector growth and intensified competition. Lotus Technology has assessed evolving market conditions and uncertainties posed by new tariff policies in the U.S. and EU, and the company has revised its delivery target for 2024 to 12,000 units. Lotus Technology launched the “Win26” plan, aiming to achieve positive EBITDA and positive operating cash flow in 2026 by further optimizing its internal processes and structures, implementing overall cost control measures, and recalibrating its product plans to cater to globally diversified markets.

How is the company's overseas expansion situation?As a legendary brand with a history of 76 years, Lotus has a high reputation in the global market. The company currently has over 200 sales outlets in key global locations. The company's global deliveries were balanced, with the four major markets (Europe, China, North America, and Rest of World) each contributing 20-30% of deliveries. The company's lifestyle vehicles (Eletre and Emeya) are gradually entering new markets. After delivery in Southeast Asia, the Eletre SUV has started delivery in markets such as the Middle East, New Zealand, India, and Egypt. Eletre SUV production orders have commenced in Australia, Japan, and South Korea. The Emeya GT sedan has started delivery in Europe and has been pre-ordered in the Middle East, Northeast Asia, and Southeast Asia.

Lotus Technology is a luxury battery electric vehicle (BEV) manufacturer that designs, develops, and sells luxury lifestyle vehicles under the iconic British brand “Lotus.” With over seven decades of heritage and proven leadership in racetrack and road cars, the Lotus brand symbolizes market-leading standards in performance, design, and engineering. The first lifestyle production vehicle, Eletre, is a new breed of pure electric SUV powered by Lotus’s proprietary 800-volt electric powertrain architecture (EPA). Deliveries of the Eletre commenced in China and Europe in 2023. Planning is underway for deliveries to the U.S. and the rest of the world. The second lifestyle production vehicle, Emeya, was launched in September 2023, and deliveries of the Emeya began in March 2024. In addition to the Eletre and Emeya, Lotus Technology plans to launch two additional fully electric vehicles in the near future, including a D-segment SUV in 2025 and a BEV sports car in 2026. Lotus Technology also plans to upgrade its models on an ongoing basis.

content: As of the date of this prospectus, Mr. Shufu Li indirectly owns more than 50% of the aggregate voting power of Lotus Technology's total issued and outstanding share capital. As a result, Lotus Technology qualifies as a “controlled company” within the meaning of Nasdaq’s corporate governance standards and has the option not to comply with certain requirements to which companies that are not controlled companies are subject, including the requirement that a majority of Lotus Technology's board of directors shall consist of independent directors and the requirement that Lotus Technology's nominating and corporate governance committee and compensation committee shall be composed entirely of independent directors. See “Risk Factors    —    Risks Relating to Our Securities    —    As a company incorporated in the Cayman Islands and a “controlled company” within the meaning of the Nasdaq corporate governance rules, Lotus Technology is permitted to adopt certain home country practices in relation to corporate governance matters that differ significantly from Nasdaq corporate governance listing standards applicable to domestic U.S. companies or rely on exemptions that are available to a “controlled company”; these practices may afford less protection to shareholders than they would enjoy if Lotus Technology complied fully with Nasdaq corporate governance listing standards.”

Lotus Technology is an exempted company incorporated in the Cayman Islands and listed on Nasdaq as a foreign private issuer. Nasdaq listing rules permit a foreign private issuer like Lotus Technology to follow the corporate governance practices of its home country. Certain corporate governance practices in the Cayman Islands, which is Lotus Technology's home country, may differ significantly from Nasdaq corporate governance listing standards applicable to domestic U.S. companies. Lotus Technology relies on the home country practice exemption available to foreign private issuers for the requirement under (i) Nasdaq Rule 5605(c)(2)(A) that each of the members of Lotus Technology's audit committee must be an independent director as defined under Nasdaq Rule 5605(a)(2) and (ii) Nasdaq Rule 5620 that each issuer must hold an annual meeting of shareholders no later than one year after the end of the issuer’s fiscal year-end. Lotus Technology may choose to follow additional home country practices in the future. Lotus Technology is a “controlled company” as defined under the Nasdaq corporate governance rules because Mr. Shufu Li owns more than 50% of Lotus Technology's total voting power as of the date of this prospectus.

Lotus Technology Inc. (“the Company”), an exempted company with limited liability, was incorporated in the Cayman Islands on August 9, 2021. The Company, through its wholly owned subsidiaries, consolidated variable interest entity (“VIE”) and VIE’s subsidiaries (collectively, “the Group”), is primarily engaged in the design, development, and sale of luxury battery electric lifestyle vehicles (“BEV lifestyle models”) under the “Lotus” brand (“Lotus BEV business”). The Group is also a distributor that sells luxury sports cars under the “Lotus” brand (“Lotus sports car business”) across the world. Additionally, the Group provides other sales and ancillary services to customers. On February 22, 2024 (the “Closing Date”), Lotus Technology consummated its merger with L Catterton Asia Acquisition Corp (“LCAA”) pursuant to a merger agreement dated January 31, 2023 (as amended and restated dated as of October 11, 2023, the “Merger Agreement”) by and among LCAA, Lotus Technology, a Cayman Islands exempted company, Lotus Temp Limited, a Cayman Islands exempted company and wholly-owned subsidiary of Lotus Technology (“Merger Sub 1”), and Lotus EV Limited, a Cayman Islands exempted company and wholly-owned subsidiary of Lotus Technology (“Merger Sub 2”).
