Lotus Technology believes this is the most efficient approach to market Lotus cars and promote the Lotus brand globally. As of December 31, 2023, Lotus Technology had 215 stores in its global distribution network, and plans to expand its retail network to over 300 stores by 2025.

Lotus Technology's ability to successfully operate its Global Commercial Platform will affect its ability to increase revenues. Lotus Technology has taken steps to diversify its revenue sources, for example, by entering into the Distribution Agreement with Lotus UK, pursuant to which Lotus Technology is the exclusive global distributor (excluding the U.S., where Lotus Technology International Limited will act as the head distributor with the existing regional distributor continuing its functions) for Lotus Cars Limited to distribute Lotus UK vehicles, parts, and certain tools, and to provide aftersales services, branding, marketing, and public relations for such vehicles, parts, and tools distributed by Lotus Technology. As of December 31, 2023, Lotus Technology had 215 stores in its global distribution network. Major vehicle models currently in Lotus Technology's global sales and distribution network include Eletre, Emeya, and future battery electric vehicle models, as well as certain sports car models that were or will be developed and manufactured by Lotus UK, such as Evija (battery electric vehicle sports car) and Emira (internal combustion engine sports car).

In particular, in China, Lotus Technology's vehicles are sold either directly to users (rather than through dealerships) or through city partners that act as sales agents and collect sales commissions on such sales. As of December 31, 2023, Lotus Technology had 215 stores in its global distribution network. Lotus Technology plans to further expand its physical sales network through a balanced combination of company-operated stores and partner stores. This planned expansion may not have the desired effect of increasing sales and enhancing brand recognition in a cost-efficient manner. Lotus Technology may need to invest significant capital and management resources to operate existing company-operated stores and open new ones, and there can be no assurance that Lotus Technology will be able to improve the operational efficiency of its company-operated stores. Lotus Technology's direct-to-consumer approach to vehicle distribution is relatively new, and its long-term effectiveness is unproven, especially in China. This approach subjects Lotus Technology to substantial risks as it requires, in the aggregate, significant expenditures and provides for slower expansion of its distribution and sales systems compared to the traditional dealership system.

The Lotus brand is closely associated with “customer engagement” and “community building,” and in order to provide customers with a luxurious experience, Lotus Technology has adopted a direct-to-consumer global sales and distribution strategy that focuses on establishing and developing direct relationships with customers, especially in selected regions including China, Europe, and the U.S., which represent some of the key areas driving fast growth in the global battery electric vehicle markets. By offering a luxurious purchasing experience and superior customer service, Lotus Technology expects to further deepen its penetration in the global market and aims to establish a total of over 300 stores globally by 2025. Develop next-generation electric vehicle technologies while monetizing Lotus Technology’s R&D prowess. Lotus Technology intends to continuously develop and enhance proprietary cutting-edge technologies, including the 800-volt EPA architecture and hardware, algorithm, and software systems to enhance the competitiveness of its vehicles, supported by continuous R&D investment. All of Lotus Technology's proprietary technologies are built with the principle of “For the Drivers,” while inheriting its unique design language and philosophy.

By building upon Lotus Technology's iconic sports car reputation, along with the ambition to invest in a lifestyle business, the company has established a strong commercial organizational competence. Lotus Technology's marketing activities include branding, global marketing campaigns, public relations, digital marketing, creative product marketing, communications, social media, and other marketing programs. The company aims to create demand and expand its customer base globally. Lotus Technology plans to build a network of around 300 retail stores globally by the end of 2025. Lotus Technology believes that one of the most effective forms of marketing is to continually improve customers’ experience. The omni-channel sales model is customer-oriented, where customers can both access the online platform to customize their vehicles, make payments, and order aftersales services, and visit in person to consult the sales team and reserve test-driving sessions. Lotus Technology's marketing department is responsible for building the brand and corporate image, promoting products, facilitating a best-in-class customer experience, and providing information regarding the company's products, strategies, and technology through the omni-channel sales model.

How is the company's overseas expansion situation?As a legendary brand with a history of 76 years, Lotus has a high reputation in the global market. The company currently has over 200 sales outlets in key global locations. The company's global deliveries were balanced, with the four major markets (Europe, China, North America, and Rest of World) each contributing 20-30% of deliveries. The company's lifestyle vehicles (Eletre and Emeya) are gradually entering new markets. After delivery in Southeast Asia, the Eletre SUV has started delivery in markets such as the Middle East, New Zealand, India, and Egypt. Eletre SUV production orders have commenced in Australia, Japan, and South Korea. The Emeya GT sedan has started delivery in Europe and has been pre-ordered in the Middle East, Northeast Asia, and Southeast Asia.

The Distribution Agreement also provides that each year Lotus Technology and Lotus UK will prepare business plans and annual targets taking into account historical sales figures, forecast demand, national, regional, and local trends, and Lotus UK’s production capacity for the vehicles. Additionally, pursuant to the Distribution Agreement, existing stores and dealers of Lotus UK are transferred to Lotus Technology. As of June 30, 2024, Lotus Technology had 207 stores in its global distribution network. Major vehicle models under the Distribution Agreement include Eletre, Emeya, and future lifestyle models, as well as certain sports car models that were or will be developed and manufactured by Lotus UK, such as Evija (BEV sports car) and Emira (ICE sports car).

Lotus Technology has taken steps to diversify its revenue sources, for example, by entering into the Distribution Agreement with Lotus UK, pursuant to which Lotus Technology is the exclusive global distributor (excluding the U.S., where Lotus Technology International Limited will act as the head distributor with the existing regional distributor continuing its functions) for Lotus Cars Limited to distribute Lotus UK vehicles, parts, and certain tools, and to provide aftersales services, branding, marketing, and public relations for such vehicles, parts, and tools distributed by Lotus Technology. As of December 31, 2023, Lotus Technology had 215 stores in its global distribution network. Major vehicle models currently in Lotus Technology's global sales and distribution network include Eletre, Emeya, and future battery electric vehicle models, as well as certain sports car models that were or will be developed and manufactured by Lotus UK, such as Evija (battery electric vehicle sports car) and Emira (internal combustion engine sports car).

What is the product plan for the future? otus Technology currently has four models on sale: the pure electric hypercar EVIJA (priced above $2.2 million), the pure electric intelligent SUV ELETRE (priced above $100,000, E-class), the pure electric intelligent GT Sedan EMEYA (priced above $100,000, E-class), and the fuel-powered sports car EMIRA (priced above $85,000). Lotus Technology plans to launch a new model each in 2025 and 2026.

The company expects to capture market share and realize first mover advantages by addressing unfilled demands in the current market. Eletre is a luxury lifestyle E-segment SUV powered by Lotus Technology's 800-volt EPA. Combining its technologically advanced platform with cutting-edge design, Eletre delivers leading performance in acceleration, driving range, and charging speed. Lotus Technology offers three different versions of Eletre, namely, Eletre, Eletre S, and Eletre R, to satisfy the various demands of customers. Eletre R, in particular, generates a maximum of 905 horsepower (hp) and can accelerate from 0 to 100 km/h in 2.95 seconds. Its 112-kWh battery pack offers a maximum WLTP range of 490 km and can be recharged from 10% to 80% in less than 20 minutes. While offering unrivaled performance, Eletre comes at a better value-for-money proposition — with an average MSRP higher than US$100,000 — compared to traditional luxury OEMs. Lotus Technology commenced deliveries of Eletre in 2023. The company's second lifestyle production vehicle, Emeya, was launched in September 2023, and Lotus Technology began deliveries of Emeya in March 2024. In addition to Eletre and Emeya, Lotus Technology plans to introduce two new models in the near future.

question: 路特斯的主要竞争优势是什么？
rewritten: What are the main competitive advantages of Lotus Technology?
answer:
主要竞争优势包括：1. 豪华电动汽车市场的先发优势；2. 标志性赛车传统；3. 专有先进技术，如800伏架构和L4级自动驾驶能力；4. 轻资产商业模式，借助吉利
资源；5. 强大的可持续发展承诺；6. 全渠道奢侈品零售模式；7. 经验丰富的领导团队。
content: Competition Lotus Technology faces competition from both traditional luxury automotive developers and an increasing number of newer companies focused on electric and other alternative fuel vehicles. Lotus Technology believes the primary competitive factors on which Lotus Technology competes with its peers include, but are not limited to: ● brand recognition, prestige, and heritage; ● design, styling, and luxury; ● technological innovation; ● driver experience; ● product quality and performance; ● product reliability and safety; ● battery range, efficiency, and charging speeds; ● customer service and customer experience, including access to charging options and availability and terms of aftersales services; ● product pricing; ● management team experience in bringing electric vehicles and other disruptive technologies to market; ● manufacturing efficiency; ● environmental impact and perception; ● the degree and sophistication of related vehicle software. Lotus Technology believes that the company is favorably positioned to compete on the basis of these factors. However, many of Lotus Technology's current and potential competitors have substantially greater financial, technical, manufacturing, marketing, and other resources than the company. These competitors may be able to deploy greater resources to the design, development, manufacturing, distribution, promotion, sales, marketing, and support of their products.

Additionally, many of these competitors also have greater name recognition, longer operating histories, larger sales forces, broader customer and industry relationships, and other tangible and intangible resources that exceed those of Lotus Technology. These competitors also compete with Lotus Technology in recruiting and retaining qualified research and development, sales, marketing, and management personnel, as well as in acquiring technologies complementary to, or necessary for, the company's products. Additional mergers and acquisitions in the electric vehicle and luxury automotive markets may result in even more resources being concentrated in Lotus Technology's competitors. Lotus Technology believes that its brand and history, focus on design and experience, advanced technologies, relationship with Geely Holding, and the benefits obtained during the vehicle development phase, along with its future-proof approach, provide a competitive edge and enable the formulation of a highly differentiated go-to-market strategy. Lotus Technology also has a scalable asset-light business model that is believed to generate significant competitive advantages, allowing the company to incur less upfront capital expenditure and focus on research and development and technologies.

Lotus Technology believes it benefits from a number of competitive advantages: Early mover in the luxury BEV market. Lotus Technology is well positioned to capitalize on the rapid growth of the global luxury BEV market and address unmet demand by offering a portfolio of BEV models. Additionally, the global luxury BEV market is underserved due to limited availability of models. As an early mover in the luxury BEV market, Lotus Technology has launched its E-segment BEV model years ahead of competitors and expects to continue launching its BEV models, anticipating significant early mover advantages in terms of electrification progress compared to other brands. Iconic brand with racing heritage. The core principles and Lotus Technology's DNA at the heart of the company come from more than 70 years of sports car design and engineering. The concepts of aerodynamics and lightweight sports car design are highly distinguished and have been an essential part of the Lotus brand’s ethos.

Since its inception, Team Lotus has won multiple Formula 1 championships, including 6 “FIA Formula 1 Drivers’ World Championships,” 7 “FIA Formula 1 Constructors’ World Championships,” and 81 “FIA Formula 1 Grand Prix Wins.” The Lotus brand was also awarded “Luxury Brand of the Year” at the prestigious “Luxury Briefing Awards 2019,” while the Evija received a “2020 MUSE Global Design Award.” Proprietary next-generation technology built on world-class research and development capabilities. Lotus Group has consistently been a technological pioneer in the automotive industry over the past seven decades. The company's exceptional technologies are demonstrated by its proprietary 800-volt EPA architecture, which entails supercharging capabilities, high energy conservation, and high-speed data transmission, with high adaptability that can accommodate varying battery sizes, motors, and component layouts across vehicle classes. Additionally, Lotus Group possesses leading Advanced Driver Assistance Systems (ADAS) technology with fully embedded Level 4-ready hardware capability, enabled by the world’s first deployable LiDAR system, five 360° perception coverage, and a self-developed software system for cognition, decision-making, design, and control algorithms. The operating system, Lotus Hyper OS, utilizes technology to create real-time 3D content for passengers.

Lotus Technology believes that its brand and history, its focus on design and experience, its advanced technologies, its relationship with Geely Holding and therefore the benefits it can obtain during the vehicle development phase, and its future-proof approach give it a competitive edge and allow it to formulate a highly differentiated go-to-market strategy. Factors affecting competition include, among others, local protectionism, brand recognition, product quality and performance, technological innovation, product design and styling, pricing, safety, and customer service. Increased competition may lead to lower vehicle unit sales and increased inventory, which may adversely affect Lotus Technology's business, financial condition, operating results, and prospects. content: The Lotus brand is closely associated with “customer engagement” and “community building,” and in order to provide customers with a luxurious experience, Lotus Technology has adopted a direct-to-consumer global sales and distribution strategy that focuses on establishing and developing direct relationships with customers, especially in selected regions including China, Europe, and the U.S., which represent some of the key areas driving fast growth in the global battery electric vehicle markets. By offering a luxurious purchasing experience and superior customer service, Lotus Technology expects to further deepen its penetration in the global market and aims to establish a total of over 300 stores globally by 2025. Develop next-generation electric vehicle technologies while monetizing Lotus Technology’s R&D prowess. All of Lotus Technology's proprietary technologies are built with the principle of “For the Drivers,” while inheriting its unique design language and philosophy.

Lotus Technology is in the process of transforming from a British sports car company to a global pioneer of high-performance lifestyle electric vehicles and has set high quality and safety standards for and makes continuous improvements on its vehicles and technologies, including sensory hardware and ADAS software. Lotus Technology also plans to monetize its R&D capabilities by licensing its intellectual properties to other luxury auto brands and providing ADAS software subscriptions to its customers. Continue to launch new models and upgrade existing models. Lotus Technology plans to launch a well-balanced portfolio of new models with exceptional performance in the coming years to broaden its customer base, in particular high net worth individuals and the tech-savvy younger generations, and expand its product breadth. Lotus Technology plans to launch two additional fully-electric vehicles over the next two years, including a D-segment SUV in 2025 and a BEV lifestyle sports car in 2026. Lotus Technology also intends to upgrade its models to equip its vehicles with innovative technology and design. Focus on sustainability and lead in electrification.

As a leader in the electrification transformation of the luxury car segment, Lotus Technology has launched its E-segment BEV model years ahead of competitors and plans to continue launching additional BEV models. Lotus Technology also aims to become the first traditional luxury auto brand to achieve a 100% BEV product portfolio by 2027. As part of its sustainability commitment, Lotus Technology aims to achieve carbon neutrality by 2038, with the Lotus Global Smart Factory, which is owned and operated by Geely Holding. Lotus Technology expects to continue focusing on sustainability leadership through waste reduction and the ongoing adoption of renewable energy and recyclable materials.

Invest in brand equity and fully transform the brand. Scale up and expand geographical presence. Lotus Technology intends to deepen its penetration across all regions. By offering a luxurious purchasing experience and superior customer service, Lotus Technology expects to further deepen its penetration in the global market. Develop next-generation electric vehicle technologies while monetizing Lotus Technology's R&D prowess.

Lotus Technology is well positioned to capitalize on the growth momentum of the global luxury battery electric vehicle (BEV) segment by leveraging the following strategies: ● Invest in brand equity and fully transform Lotus Technology's brand. ● Scale up and expand Lotus Technology's geographical presence. ● Develop next-generation electric vehicle technologies while monetizing Lotus Technology's R&D prowess. ● Continue to launch new vehicle models and upgrade existing models. ● Lotus Technology will focus on sustainability and lead in electrification.

To help engage the next generation of Lotus colleagues, Lotus Technology leverages its unique global appeal and stimulates the ambitions of the next generation, especially in the creative science, technology, engineering, art, and mathematics subjects. Lotus Technology helps carry out educational outreach programs and works with communities outside the core business to provide support and stability within those environments. Lotus Technology also adopts and aligns its strategy to external frameworks including the UN Global Compact, including commitments to transparent, responsible, and ethical business management. Lotus Technology released its first annual ESG Report on October 25, 2023, which documented its ESG strategy and highlighted its 2022 ESG achievements as part of Lotus’s advancement into a fully electric, intelligent, and sustainable luxury mobility provider under the brand’s Vision80 strategy. The strategy focuses on six key fields: Nature Positive, Climate Neutrality, Sustainable Supply Chain, Inclusion and Equality, Community Commitment, and Transparent Governance. Lotus Technology joined the United Nations Global Compact (UNGC) in May 2023 and has set targets aligned with the United Nations Sustainable Development Goals (SDGs) for each field, embedding actionable frameworks into its operations to achieve the global goals.

Lotus Technology plans to launch a well-balanced portfolio of new models with exceptional performance in the coming years to broaden its customer base, in particular high net worth individuals and the tech-savvy younger generations, and expand its product breadth. Lotus Technology plans to introduce two new models in the near future. Lotus Technology also intends to upgrade its models to equip its vehicles with innovative technology and design. Focus on sustainability and lead in electrification. As a leader in the electrification transformation of the luxury car segment, Lotus Technology has launched its E-segment battery electric vehicle (BEV) model years ahead of its competitors and plans to continue launching its other BEV models. As part of its sustainability commitment, Lotus Technology aims to achieve carbon neutrality by 2038. Lotus Technology expects to continue focusing on sustainability leadership through waste reduction and the ongoing adoption of renewable energy and recyclable materials. In addition, Lotus Technology launched the “Win26” plan, aiming to achieve positive EBITDA in 2026 by further optimizing its internal processes and structures, implementing overall cost measures, and recalibrating its product plans to cater to globally diversified markets.

Fusing proprietary next-generation technology built on world-class research and development capabilities and an asset-light model empowered by Geely Holding, Lotus Technology is breaking new ground in electrification, digitization, and intelligence. The Lotus brand was founded in the U.K. in 1948 and has since established itself as a high-performance sports car brand with innovative engineering and cutting-edge technologies, renowned for its superior aerodynamics and lightweight design. The Lotus brand was born out of legendary success on the racetrack, including 13 FIA Formula 1 world titles and many other championship honors. In 2017, Geely Holding acquired a 51% stake in Lotus UK and further positioned the company as a luxury lifestyle battery electric vehicle (BEV) maker.

question: 2024年9月27日LTIL和Geely UK Limited之间签署的股权转让协议，请简述协议内容
rewritten: Please summarize the content of the share transfer agreement signed between LTIL and Geely UK Limited on September 27, 2024
answer:
2024年9月27日，Lotus Technology Innovative Limited（以下简称“LTIL”）与Geely UK Limited（以下简称
“Geely”）签订了一份股份出售和转让协议（以下简称“Share Transfer Agreement”）。根据该协议，LTIL同意出售，Geely同意购买L
otus Tech Innovation Center GmbH（以下简称“LTIC”）的800,000股股份，交易金额为10,880,000欧元。LTIC是一
家在德国注册的公司，是LTIL的全资子公司。该交易在签署股份转让协议时同时完成。交易完成后，Geely将持有LTIC 80%的股份。此信息基于2024年10月0
1日前的知识。
content: Lotus Technology Innovative Limited (the “LTIL”), a wholly-owned subsidiary of Lotus Technology Inc., recently entered into a share sale and transfer agreement (the “Share Transfer Agreement”) with Geely UK Limited (“Geely”), pursuant to which LTIL agrees to sell, and Geely agrees to purchase, 800,000 shares in Lotus Tech Innovation Center GmbH (“LTIC”), a company incorporated in Germany and a wholly-owned subsidiary of LTIL, for an aggregate principal amount of EUR 10,880,000 (the “Transaction”). The Transaction closed simultaneously with the signing of the Share Transfer Agreement. Upon completion of the Transaction, Geely beneficially owns 80% of LTIC’s share capital. A copy of the Share Transfer Agreement is included in this current report on Form 6-K as Exhibit 10.1, and the foregoing description of the Share Transfer Agreement is qualified in its entirety by reference thereto. Share Sale and Transfer Agreement, dated September 27, 2024, by and between Lotus Technology Innovative Limited and Geely UK Limited.

question: 介绍一下Kershaw Health Limited
rewritten: Can you provide an introduction to Kershaw Health Limited?
answer:
Kershaw Health Limited 是一家在英格兰和威尔士注册的私人有限公司。根据2024年11月7日与Lotus Technology Inc.签订
的债券认购协议，Kershaw Health Limited同意认购并购买Lotus Technology Inc.发行的2029年到期的高级债券，本金金额为5
亿美元。Kershaw Health Limited需在债券发行日期后的30天内支付全部认购价格。 以上信息基于2024年11月8日之前的资料。
content: THIS SUBSCRIPTION AGREEMENT (this “Agreement”), dated November 7, 2024, is entered into by and between (i) Lotus Technology Inc., an exempted company incorporated in the Cayman Islands (the “Company”), and Kershaw Health Limited, a private limited company incorporated in England and Wales (the “Subscriber”). Each of the Company and the Subscriber is referred to herein individually as a “Party” and collectively as the “Parties.”
Bond Subscription Agreement, dated November 7, 2024, by and between Lotus Technology Inc. and Kershaw Health Limited, Form of Global Certificate.

On November 7, 2024, Lotus Technology entered into a bond subscription agreement (the “Bond Subscription Agreement”) with Kershaw Health Limited, a private limited company incorporated in England and Wales (the “Subscriber”), pursuant to which the Subscriber agrees to subscribe for and purchase, and Lotus Technology hereby agrees to issue and sell to the Subscriber, a senior bond due 2029 (the “Bond”), with the principal amount of US$500,000,000, at a subscription price of 100% of the principal amount of the Bond. The Subscriber shall pay or cause to be paid the subscription price in the amount of US$500,000,000 to Lotus Technology within thirty (30) days after the issue date of the Bond. Pursuant to the Bond Subscription Agreement, prior to the purchase price of the Bonds being paid in full by the Subscriber, Lotus Technology may at its option repurchase the Bond, in whole but not in part, at a repurchase price equal to the aggregate principal amount of the Bond paid by the Subscriber to the repurchase date (if any).

This Bond was issued under a Subscription Agreement, dated as of [●], 2024 between Lotus Technology Inc., an exempted company incorporated in the Cayman Islands (the “Issuer”) with its principal place of business at No. 800 Century Avenue, Pudong District, Shanghai, People’s Republic of China, and Kershaw Health Limited, a private limited company incorporated in England and Wales (the “Bondholder”). Solely between the Issuer and the Bondholder, the terms of the Bond include those stated in the Subscription Agreement. The Bond is subject to all such terms, and holders are referred to the Subscription Agreement for a statement of all such terms. To the extent permitted by applicable law, in the event of any inconsistency between the terms of this Bond and the terms of the Subscription Agreement, the terms of the Subscription Agreement will control. For the avoidance of doubt, the Agents will not be bound by or have notice of any of the terms and provisions of the Subscription Agreement.

Copies of the Bond Subscription Agreement and the Form of Global Certificate are included in this current report on Form 6-K as Exhibit 10.1 and Exhibit 10.2, respectively, and the foregoing description of the Bond Subscription Agreement and the Bond is qualified in its entirety by reference thereto.

question: 公司对全球豪华电动车市场持什么观点？对行业竞争怎么看？
rewritten: What is the company's view on the global luxury electric vehicle market? What is the company's perspective on industry competition?
answer:
Lotus Technology 预计全球豪华电动汽车市场将快速增长，从2021年到2031年的复合年增长率将达到35%，到2031年市场将达到近190万辆。然
而，目前市场供应不足，只有大约10款豪华电动汽车在售，而传统内燃机豪华车型则超过100款，消费者选择有限。作为早期进入者，Lotus Technology 通过
推出其首款全电动高性能SUV Eletre，领先于竞争对手，旨在填补市场空白并抓住先发优势。Eletre 提供三种不同版本：Eletre、Eletre S 和 
Eletre R，其中 Eletre R 最大功率可达905马力，0到100公里/小时加速仅需2.95秒。公司在行业竞争中面临的主要因素包括品牌认知、声望和历史
；设计、风格和豪华感；技术创新；驾驶体验；产品质量和性能；产品可靠性和安全性；电池续航、效率和充电速度；客户服务和体验，包括充电选项的可获得性和售后服务的可用性
和条款；产品定价；管理团队在将电动汽车和其他颠覆性技术推向市场的经验；制造效率；环境影响和认知；以及相关车辆软件的复杂程度。尽管如此，许多当前和潜在的竞争对手拥
有远超 Lotus Technology 的财务、技术、制造、营销和其他资源，这些竞争对手可能能够投入更多资源用于设计、开发、制造、分销、推广、销售和产品支持。
此外，许多竞争对手还拥有更高的品牌认知度、更长的经营历史、更大的销售团队、更广泛的客户和行业关系，以及其他有形和无形资源。Lotus Technology 预期
未来汽车行业竞争将加剧，特别是在替代燃料车辆需求增加和全球汽车行业整合的背景下。这些竞争因素可能会影响公司的业务、财务状况、经营成果和前景。以上信息基于2024
年9月19日之前的认知。
content: The growth of the global luxury car segment is expected to outpace that of the overall car market, growing at a CAGR of 10% from 2021 to 2031, according to Oliver Wyman. Additionally, driven by regulatory tailwinds and increasing sustainability awareness, the total global battery electric vehicle (BEV) market is expected to grow rapidly at a CAGR of 24% from 2021 to 2031. Within the global BEV market, the luxury BEV segment is expected to outgrow the mass market BEV segment, growing at a CAGR of 35% and reaching a total market size of 1.9 million units by 2025. Lotus Technology is well positioned to capitalize on the growth momentum of the global luxury BEV segment by leveraging the following strategies. Invest in brand equity and fully transform the brand. Leveraging Lotus Technology's racing heritage and proven leadership, the company intends to further elevate the brand by continuing to deliver a portfolio of fully electric and high-performance vehicles that satisfy customers’ expectations and broaden the customer base, particularly high net worth individuals and tech-savvy younger generations who value the brand’s DNA. Scale up and expand geographical presence. Lotus Technology intends to deepen its penetration across all regions.

The global luxury battery electric vehicle (BEV) market, as defined by BEVs with a manufacturer's suggested retail price (MSRP) of over US$80,000, is expected to grow rapidly. However, the global luxury BEV market is currently underserved, with only approximately 10 existing luxury BEV models, compared to over 100 internal combustion engine (ICE) luxury models, leaving consumers with limited choices. As an early mover in the global luxury BEV market, Lotus Technology is leading the electrification transformation of this fast-growing luxury car segment, launching its E-segment BEV model years ahead of competitors. Lotus Technology launched its first fully electric Hyper-SUV, the Eletre, in 2022. The company expects to capture market share and realize first mover advantages by addressing unfilled demands in the current market. Eletre is a luxury lifestyle E-segment SUV powered by Lotus Technology's 800-volt EPA. Combining its technologically advanced platform with cutting-edge design, Eletre delivers leading performance in acceleration, driving range, and charging speed. Lotus Technology offers three different versions of Eletre, namely, Eletre, Eletre S, and Eletre R, to satisfy the various demands of customers. Eletre R, in particular, generates a maximum of 905 horsepower (hp) and can accelerate from 0 to 100 km/h in 2.95 seconds.

This relationship provides L Catterton with direct access to LVMH’s most senior executives, global consumer insights, industry network and contacts, differentiated investment deal flow, and collaboration on operating best practices. L Catterton retains sole discretion over all investment and portfolio management decisions related to the L Catterton funds. As of the date of this prospectus, Lotus Technology has not entered into any agreements with LVMH. According to Oliver Wyman, the global luxury battery electric vehicle (BEV) market, as defined by BEVs with a manufacturer's suggested retail price (MSRP) of over US$80,000, is expected to grow rapidly at a compound annual growth rate (CAGR) of 35% from 2021 to 2031 and reach a market size of nearly 1.9 million units by 2031. However, the global luxury BEV market is currently underserved, with only approximately 10 existing luxury BEV models, compared to over 100 internal combustion engine (ICE) luxury models, leaving consumers with limited choices.

However, the global luxury BEV market is currently underserved, with only approximately 10 existing luxury BEV models, compared to over 100 internal combustion engine (ICE) luxury models, leaving consumers with limited choices. As an early mover in the global luxury BEV market, Lotus Technology is leading the electrification transformation of this fast-growing luxury car segment, launching its E-segment BEV model years ahead of competitors and targeting to become the first traditional luxury auto brand to achieve a 100% BEV product portfolio by 2027. Lotus Technology launched its first fully electric Hyper-SUV, Eletre, in 2022. Beginning with Eletre, all new car rollouts will be BEV models. Lotus Technology expects to capture market share and realize first mover advantages by addressing unfilled demands in the current market. Eletre is a luxury lifestyle E-segment SUV powered by Lotus Technology's 800-volt Electrical Performance Architecture, or EPA, which is a newly debuted self-developed BEV platform initially based on the same foundation of Sustainable Experience Architecture, the world’s first open-source BEV architecture. Combining its technologically advanced platform with cutting-edge design, Eletre delivers leading performance in acceleration, driving range, and charging speed.

The global automotive market is highly competitive and has historically been associated with significant barriers to entry, including large capital requirements and investment costs for developing, designing, manufacturing, and distributing vehicles, long lead times to bring vehicles to market from the concept and design stage, the need for specialized design and development expertise, regulatory requirements, establishing a brand name and image, and the need to establish sales and service locations. Lotus Technology has strategically entered into the sustainable luxury battery electric vehicle (BEV) market, and the company faces a variety of added challenges that a traditional automobile manufacturer would not encounter, including additional costs of developing and producing an electric powertrain that has comparable performance to a traditional gasoline engine in terms of range and power, inexperience with servicing electric vehicles, regulations associated with the transport of batteries, and the need to establish or provide access to sufficient charging locations and unproven high-volume customer demand for fully electric vehicles. Lotus Technology expects the sustainable luxury BEV market segment to become even more competitive in the future as additional players enter this segment. The company competes with competitors all around the world.

Lotus Technology believes it benefits from a number of competitive advantages: ● Early mover in the luxury battery electric vehicle (BEV) market.  
● Iconic brand with a racing heritage.  
● Proprietary next-generation technology built on world-class research and development (R&D) capabilities.  
● Asset-light business model supported by the Geely Holding ecosystem.  
● Focus on sustainability targeting an intelligent and high-performance product portfolio.  
● Luxury retail experience and an omni-channel sales model.  
● Global, experienced, and visionary leadership. Lotus Technology is well positioned to capitalize on the growth momentum of the global luxury battery electric vehicle (BEV) segment by leveraging the following strategies:

As of June 30, 2024, Lotus Technology had 207 stores in its global distribution network, and it plans to continue expanding its retail network. In 2023 and the six months ended June 30, 2024, Lotus Technology delivered a total of 6,970 and 4,873 vehicles, respectively, consisting of lifestyle vehicles and Lotus-brand sports cars that were distributed through Lotus Technology International Limited (LTIL). The growth of the global luxury car segment is expected to outpace that of the overall car market. Within the global battery electric vehicle (BEV) market, the luxury BEV segment is expected to outgrow the mass market BEV segment. Lotus Technology is well positioned to capitalize on the growth momentum of the global luxury BEV segment by leveraging the following strategies.

Competition Lotus Technology faces competition from both traditional luxury automotive developers and an increasing number of newer companies focused on electric and other alternative fuel vehicles. Lotus Technology believes the primary competitive factors on which Lotus Technology competes with its peers include, but are not limited to: ● brand recognition, prestige, and heritage; ● design, styling, and luxury; ● technological innovation; ● driver experience; ● product quality and performance; ● product reliability and safety; ● battery range, efficiency, and charging speeds; ● customer service and customer experience, including access to charging options and availability and terms of aftersales services; ● product pricing; ● management team experience in bringing electric vehicles and other disruptive technologies to market; ● manufacturing efficiency; ● environmental impact and perception; ● the degree and sophistication of related vehicle software. Lotus Technology believes that the company is favorably positioned to compete on the basis of these factors. However, many of Lotus Technology's current and potential competitors have substantially greater financial, technical, manufacturing, marketing, and other resources than the company. These competitors may be able to deploy greater resources to the design, development, manufacturing, distribution, promotion, sales, marketing, and support of their products.

These competitors also compete with Lotus Technology in recruiting and retaining qualified research and development, sales, marketing, and management personnel, as well as in acquiring technologies complementary to, or necessary for, its products. Additional mergers and acquisitions in the electric vehicle and luxury automotive markets may result in even more resources being concentrated in these competitors. Lotus Technology believes that its brand and history, its focus on design and experience, its advanced technologies, its relationship with Geely Holding and therefore the benefits it can obtain during the vehicle development phase, and its future-proof approach give it a competitive edge and allow it to formulate a highly differentiated go-to-market strategy. Lotus Technology also has a scalable asset-light business model that it believes generates significant competitive advantages, allowing it to incur less upfront capital expenditure and focus on research and development and technologies.

Many of Lotus Technology's current and potential competitors, particularly international competitors, have significantly greater financial, technical, manufacturing, marketing, and other resources than Lotus Technology does and may be able to devote greater resources to the design, development, manufacturing, distribution, promotion, sale, and support of their products. Lotus Technology expects competition in the automotive industry to intensify in the future in light of increased demand and regulatory push for alternative fuel vehicles, continuing globalization, and consolidation in the worldwide automotive industry. Factors affecting competition include, among others, local protectionism, brand recognition, product quality and performance, technological innovation, product design and styling, pricing, safety, and customer service. Increased competition may lead to lower vehicle unit sales and increased inventory, which may adversely affect Lotus Technology's business, financial condition, operating results, and prospects. The company's ability to successfully overcome the industry barriers and compete in its market will be fundamental to its future success in existing and new markets and its market share. There can be no assurance that Lotus Technology will be able to successfully overcome the industry barriers and compete in its markets.

Lotus Technology's vehicles also compete with internal combustion engine (ICE) vehicles as well as new energy vehicles. Many of Lotus Technology's current and potential competitors, particularly international competitors, have significantly greater financial, technical, manufacturing, marketing, and other resources than Lotus Technology does and may be able to devote greater resources to the design, development, manufacturing, distribution, promotion, sale, and support of their products. Lotus Technology expects competition in the automotive industry to intensify in the future in light of increased demand and regulatory push for alternative fuel vehicles, continuing globalization, and consolidation in the worldwide automotive industry. Factors affecting competition include, among others, local protectionism, brand recognition, product quality and performance, technological innovation, product design and styling, pricing, safety, and customer service. Increased competition may lead to lower vehicle unit sales and increased inventory, which may adversely affect Lotus Technology's business, financial condition, operating results, and prospects. The company's ability to successfully overcome the industry barriers and compete in its market will be fundamental to its future success in existing and new markets and its market share. There can be no assurance that Lotus Technology will be able to successfully overcome the industry barriers and compete in its markets.

Additionally, many of these competitors also have greater name recognition, longer operating histories, larger sales forces, broader customer and industry relationships, and other tangible and intangible resources that exceed those of Lotus Technology. These competitors also compete with Lotus Technology in recruiting and retaining qualified research and development, sales, marketing, and management personnel, as well as in acquiring technologies complementary to, or necessary for, the company's products. Additional mergers and acquisitions in the electric vehicle and luxury automotive markets may result in even more resources being concentrated in Lotus Technology's competitors. Lotus Technology believes that its brand and history, focus on design and experience, advanced technologies, relationship with Geely Holding, and the benefits obtained during the vehicle development phase, along with its future-proof approach, provide a competitive edge and enable the formulation of a highly differentiated go-to-market strategy. Lotus Technology also has a scalable asset-light business model that is believed to generate significant competitive advantages, allowing the company to incur less upfront capital expenditure and focus on research and development and technologies.

question: 三款Eletre有什么区别
rewritten: What are the differences between the three versions of the Eletre?
answer:
Eletre 提供了三种不同的版本，分别是 Eletre、Eletre S 和 Eletre R，以满足不同客户的需求。1. **Eletre** 和 **El
etre S**：   - **动力系统**：这两款版本均采用 450 千瓦单速版本，配备 112 千瓦时电池包。   - **续航里程**：根据 WLTP 标
准，最大续航里程为 600 公里。   - **性能**：Eletre 和 Eletre S 可以在 4.5 秒内从 0 加速到 100 公里/小时，从 80 
到 120 公里/小时的加速时间不到 2.2 秒，最大扭矩为 710 牛米。   - **定位**：Eletre S 更注重提供更长的续航里程和更高的舒适性。2
. **Eletre R**：   - **动力系统**：Eletre R 采用 675 千瓦双速系统，同样配备 112 千瓦时电池包。   - **续航里程*
*：根据 WLTP 标准，最大续航里程为 490 公里。   - **性能**：Eletre R 可以在 2.95 秒内从 0 加速到 100 公里/小时，从 
80 到 120 公里/小时的加速时间不到 1.9 秒，最大扭矩为 985 牛米，最大马力为 905 马力。   - **定位**：Eletre R 针对追求速
度和操控性能的客户，提供额外的赛道模式。这些版本在性能、续航里程和配置上有所不同，以满足不同客户的需求。以上信息基于 2024 年 9 月 19 日前的知识。
content: The company also plans to upgrade its models on an ongoing basis. The Eletre is the first of Lotus Technology's new breed of pure electric SUVs. It is the company's first lifestyle vehicle, which aims to set the standard for future lifestyle vehicles. The five-seater (four-seater as optional) measures 5,103 millimeters in length with a 3,019-millimeter wheelbase, providing customers with a spacious and comfortable riding experience. The Eletre comes with five standard drive modes, switchable by adjusting the front and rear wheel steering, damper settings, chassis control system, propulsion strategies, and accelerator pedal response. Three different versions of the Eletre are available, namely, the Eletre, the Eletre S, and the Eletre R, with the choice of two powertrains. These versions are designed to satisfy the various demands of customers — the Eletre R is tailored for customers who seek speed and control, while the Eletre S provides a longer range and more comfort. With an average MSRP higher than US$100,000, the Eletre and the Eletre S feature the 450 kilowatt single-speed version, with a maximum range of 600 kilometers (km) per full charge according to the Worldwide Harmonized Light Vehicles Test Procedure (WLTP).

Eletre is a luxury lifestyle E-segment SUV powered by Lotus Technology's 800-volt EPA, which is a newly debuted self-developed BEV platform initially based on the same foundation of Sustainable Experience Architecture, the world’s first open-source BEV architecture. Combining its technologically advanced platform with cutting-edge design, Eletre delivers leading performance in acceleration, driving range, and charging speed. Lotus Technology offers three different versions of Eletre, namely, Eletre, Eletre S, and Eletre R, to satisfy the various demands of customers. Eletre R, in particular, generates a maximum of 905 horsepower (hp) and can accelerate from 0 to 100 km/h in 2.95 seconds. Its 112-kWh battery pack offers a maximum WLTP range of 490 km and can be recharged from 10% to 80% in less than 20 minutes. While offering unrivaled performance, Eletre comes at a better value-for-money proposition — with an average MSRP higher than US$100,000 — compared to traditional luxury OEMs. Lotus Technology commenced deliveries of Eletre in China and Europe in 2023, and planning is underway for deliveries to the U.S. and the rest of the world. Lotus Technology's second lifestyle production vehicle, Emeya, was launched in September 2023, and deliveries of Emeya began in March 2024.

Its 112-kWh battery pack offers a maximum WLTP range of 490 km and can be recharged from 10% to 80% in less than 20 minutes. While offering unrivaled performance, Eletre comes at a better value-for-money proposition — with an average MSRP higher than US$100,000 — compared to traditional luxury original equipment manufacturers (OEMs). Deliveries of Eletre commenced in China and Europe in 2023. Planning is underway for deliveries to the U.S. and the rest of the world. The company expects to capture market share and realize first mover advantages by addressing unfilled demands in the current market. Combining its technologically advanced platform with cutting-edge design, Eletre delivers leading performance in acceleration, driving range, and charging speed. Its 112-kWh battery pack offers a maximum WLTP range of 490 km and can be recharged from 10% to 80% in less than 20 minutes. The Eletre and the Eletre S can deliver a top speed of 258 km per hour and an acceleration from zero to 100 km per hour in 4.5 seconds and from 80 to 120 km per hour in less than 2.2 seconds, with a maximum of 710 Newton-meters (Nm) of torque. With an average MSRP higher than US$130,000, the Eletre R comes with the flagship 675 kilowatt dual-speed system, with a maximum WLTP range of 490 km and a dual-speed version on the rear engine. The Eletre R can deliver a top speed of 265 km per hour and an acceleration from zero to 100 km per hour in just around 2.95 seconds and from 80 to 120 km per hour in less than around 1.9 seconds, with a maximum of 985 Nm of torque. In addition to the standard five drive modes, the Eletre R also comes with an additional track mode. All three versions come with a 112-kilowatt-hour battery pack, with a fast charging time of less than 20 minutes from 10% to 80% capacity using a rapid charger.

content: Lotus Technology's ability to successfully operate its Global Commercial Platform will affect its ability to increase revenues. Lotus Technology has taken steps to diversify its revenue sources, for example, by entering into the Distribution Agreement with Lotus UK, pursuant to which Lotus Technology is the exclusive global distributor (excluding the U.S., where Lotus Technology International Limited will act as the head distributor with the existing regional distributor continuing its functions) for Lotus Cars Limited to distribute Lotus UK vehicles, parts, and certain tools, and to provide aftersales services, branding, marketing, and public relations for such vehicles, parts, and tools distributed by Lotus Technology. As of December 31, 2023, Lotus Technology had 215 stores in its global distribution network. Major vehicle models currently in Lotus Technology's global sales and distribution network include Eletre, Emeya, and future battery electric vehicle models, as well as certain sports car models that were or will be developed and manufactured by Lotus UK, such as Evija (battery electric vehicle sports car) and Emira (internal combustion engine sports car).

Which remains an intrinsic part of the company's sports car DNA. In this instance, that innovative vehicle architecture was wrapped with a bold and sculpted design using cues clearly developed from the Evija. With the first customer cars scheduled for delivery in the spring of 2022, the biggest surprise on the Emira was that there would be a choice of two petrol engines from different manufacturers, both globally respected for their performance characteristics. The first 'Launch' cars would be available as limited-production 'First Edition' models powered by the much-loved and Lotus-honed 3.5-litre, Toyota-derived V6 engine, which had cut its Hethel teeth powering the Evora and Exige. But, from the summer of 2022, the first new Lotus sports car engine for more than a decade would make its debut, with the new Emira being powered by AMG.

The new-to-Lotus AMG unit was to be a significant addition. With the news that it would be the last new internal combustion engine-powered (ICE) vehicle to emerge from Hethel, on July 6th, 2021, Lotus unveiled the Lotus Emira. It featured all the hallmarks that the automotive world had come to expect from a Lotus: striking design, thrilling dynamic performance, best-in-class ride and handling, and outstanding aerodynamics.

Emira

The last new internal combustion engine-powered (ICE) vehicle to emerge from Hethel was unveiled by Lotus on July 6th, 2021. It featured all the hallmarks that the automotive world had come to expect from a Lotus: striking design, thrilling dynamic performance, best-in-class ride and handling, and outstanding aerodynamics. The Lotus Emira, Type 131, is powered by a turbocharged 2.0-litre four-cylinder 'i4' from the performance division of Mercedes-Benz. Working with Lotus for the first time, AMG came on board as a technical partner on the Emira program with some fundamental changes made to the in-line four-cylinder setup for its Lotus debut. Transverse-mounted and delivering rear-wheel drive, the unit was modified with a new air intake system and new exhaust to suit the character and mid-engined format of the Emira.

Following more than £100 million of new investment in the company's UK facilities, manufacturing of the Emira was set to take place at the home of Lotus in Hethel, Norfolk. The highly skilled operations teams were to be supported by new processes and technologies to enhance production efficiency, volume capability, and overall quality. Matt Windle added: "The Lotus Emira is the most accomplished Lotus road car ever to roll through the Hethel factory gates. Beautifully proportioned."

What is the product plan for the future? otus Technology currently has four models on sale: the pure electric hypercar EVIJA (priced above $2.2 million), the pure electric intelligent SUV ELETRE (priced above $100,000, E-class), the pure electric intelligent GT Sedan EMEYA (priced above $100,000, E-class), and the fuel-powered sports car EMIRA (priced above $85,000). Lotus Technology plans to launch a new model each in 2025 and 2026.

Lotus Technology has strategically entered into the sustainable luxury battery electric vehicle (BEV) market, and the company faces a variety of added challenges that a traditional automobile manufacturer would not encounter, including additional costs of developing and producing an electric powertrain that has comparable performance to a traditional gasoline engine in terms of range and power, inexperience with servicing electric vehicles, regulations associated with the transport of batteries, and the need to establish or provide access to sufficient charging locations and unproven high-volume customer demand for fully electric vehicles. Lotus Technology expects the sustainable luxury BEV market segment to become even more competitive in the future as additional players enter this segment. The company competes with competitors all around the world. Lotus Technology's vehicles also compete with internal combustion engine (ICE) vehicles as well as new energy vehicles.

content: Lotus Technology’s involvement with the Variable Interest Entities (VIEs) under the VIE Arrangements affected Lotus Technology’s consolidated financial position, consolidated and combined results of operations, and cash flows as indicated below. Pursuant to the Restructuring, Lotus Technology ceased to consolidate the Variable Interest Entities (VIEs) on June 30, 2023. The following consolidated assets and liabilities information of Lotus Technology’s VIEs as of December 31, 2022, and consolidated revenues, net loss, and cash flow information for the years ended December 31, 2023, 2022, and 2021 have been included in the accompanying consolidated and combined financial statements. As mentioned in note 21, on January 31, 2023, Group Lotus Limited entered into a distribution agreement with Lotus Cars Limited (“LCL”), a wholly owned subsidiary of Lotus Group International Limited (LGIL) and ultimately controlled by the Controlling Shareholder of the Company. Pursuant to this agreement, Group Lotus Limited was appointed as the exclusive global distributor (excluding the United States of America) to distribute certain models of vehicles produced by LCL and to provide after-sale services as well as brand, marketing, and public relations for such vehicles. Since early 2023, Lotus Technology Group has implemented a series of transactions to restructure its organization and business operations (the “Restructuring”).

In connection with the Restructuring, the WFOE, the VIE, and nominee equity holders of the VIE entered into a series of agreements (“VIE Restructuring Agreements”), pursuant to which, i) the WFOE acquired 100% equity interest in Sanya Lotus Venture Capital Co., Ltd. (“Hangzhou Lotus”), subsidiaries of the VIE, in February 2023; ii) all the VIE’s assets and liabilities (excluding the ICP license), business contracts, intellectual properties, and employees were transferred to a subsidiary of the WFOE at nil consideration; iii) the VIE Arrangements were terminated on June 30, 2023. In connection with the Restructuring, the Wholly Foreign-Owned Enterprise (WFOE), the former Variable Interest Entity (VIE), and nominee shareholders of the former VIE entered into the VIE Restructuring Agreements in early 2023, pursuant to which, (i) the WFOE acquired 100% equity interest in Sanya Lotus Venture Capital Co., Ltd. and Hangzhou Lotus Technology Service Co., Ltd., each being a subsidiary of the former VIE; (ii) except for the ICP license, all of the former VIE’s assets and liabilities, including its business contracts, intellectual properties, and employees, were transferred to the WFOE or subsidiaries of the WFOE at nil consideration; and (iii) the VIE Agreements were terminated.

As of the date of this prospectus, Lotus Technology's operations in mainland China are conducted by its mainland China subsidiaries, and Lotus Technology does not have any VIE structure. Lotus Technology believes that the Restructuring did not and will not have any material impact on its operations and financial results. Lotus Technology faces various risks and uncertainties related to conducting business in mainland China. Lotus Technology's business operations are primarily conducted in mainland China, and the company is subject to complex and evolving laws and regulations in mainland China. For example, Lotus Technology faces risks associated with regulatory approvals on offshore offerings, antimonopoly regulatory actions, and oversight on cybersecurity and data privacy, which may impact the company's ability to conduct certain businesses, accept foreign investments, or list on a U.S. stock exchange. These risks could result in a material adverse change in Lotus Technology's operations and the value of its securities, significantly limit or completely hinder the company's ability to continue to offer securities to investors, or cause the value of such securities to significantly decline.

Prior to May 2023, LTC relied on contractual arrangements among Wuhan Lotus Technology Limited Company, or the WFOE, Wuhan Lotus E-commerce Co., Ltd., the former variable interest entity which is referred to as the “former VIE,” and its nominee shareholders to direct the business operations of the former VIE and its subsidiaries. Since early 2023, LTC has been implementing a series of transactions to restructure its organization and business operations, which is referred to as the “Restructuring.” In connection with the Restructuring, the WFOE, the former VIE, and nominee equity holders of the former VIE entered into a series of agreements, or the VIE Restructuring Agreements, pursuant to which the WFOE acquired 100% equity interest in the former VIE’s subsidiaries. As of the date of this prospectus, Lotus Technology's operations in mainland China are conducted by its subsidiaries, and it does not have any VIE structure. The holding company structure involves unique risks to investors. As a holding company, LTC may rely on dividends from its subsidiaries for cash requirements, including any payment of dividends to its shareholders.

During the year ended December 31, 2023, Lotus Technology has implemented the Restructuring to reorganize its organization and business operations. In connection with the Restructuring, the Wholly Foreign-Owned Enterprise (WFOE), the former Variable Interest Entity (VIE), and nominee equity holders of the former VIE entered into the VIE Restructuring Agreements, pursuant to which, (i) the WFOE acquired 100% equity interest in Sanya Lotus Venture Capital Co., Ltd. and Hangzhou Lotus Technology Service Co., Ltd., subsidiaries of the former VIE, in February 2023; (ii) all the former VIE’s assets and liabilities (excluding the ICP license), business contracts, intellectual properties, and employees were transferred to a subsidiary of the WFOE at nil consideration; and (iii) the VIE structure was terminated on June 30, 2023. As of the date of this prospectus, Lotus Technology Capital’s operations in mainland China are conducted by its mainland China subsidiaries, and Lotus Technology Capital does not have any VIE structure. Lotus Technology Capital believes that the Restructuring did not and will not have any material impact on its operations and financial results.

Over 7,600 vehicles were delivered in the first nine months of 2024, a year-on-year increase of 136%. Total revenue reached $653 million, more than doubling year-on-year. Revenue from the intelligent driving business generated from customers other than Lotus surged to $11 million, with a year-on-year growth of 450%. Lotus Technology launched Hyper Hybrid EV technology that provides high performance with a combined range of over 1,100 kilometers. New York – Nov 21, 2024 – Lotus Technology Inc. (“Lotus Tech” or the “Company”), a leading global intelligent and luxury mobility provider, today announced its unaudited financial results for the third quarter and first nine months ended September 30, 2024.

New York – Oct 17, 2024 – Lotus Technology Inc. (“Lotus” or the “Company”), a leading global intelligent and luxury mobility provider, today announced its preliminary vehicle delivery results for the first nine months of 2024. Lotus Technology Inc. continues to achieve top-rate growth among traditional luxury car brands, delivering 7,543 vehicles in the first nine months of 2024, representing a year-on-year increase of 134%. Global allocation in terms of deliveries remained well-distributed, with Europe, China, North America, and the Rest of the World regions each contributing about 20-35% of the total deliveries. In the third quarter of 2024, the Emeya (GT, Sedan) was delivered to Europe, whilst the Eletre (SUV) reached markets including the GCC, Japan, and Korea. In September, Lotus Technology Inc. revealed the Eletre Carbon, a high-performance variant tailored for the North American market with a starting price of $229,900. The deliveries of the Eletre Carbon are expected to commence in the first half of 2025. Deliveries of bespoke and collection versions of both the Eletre (SUV) and the Emeya (GT, Sedan) also commenced in the third quarter.

In 2023 and the six months ended June 30, 2024, Lotus Technology delivered a total of 6,970 and 4,873 vehicles, respectively, consisting of lifestyle vehicles and Lotus-brand sports cars that were distributed through Lotus Technology International Limited (LTIL). As of June 30, 2024, Lotus Technology had 2,989 full-time employees globally, including in China and Europe, among which approximately 54.6% belong to the R&D division. The results of operations are affected by the following company-specific factors. The results of operations depend significantly on Lotus Technology's ability to achieve vehicle delivery targets, which impacts vehicle sales revenue. It is critical for Lotus Technology to successfully manage production ramp-up and quality control, in cooperation with Geely Holding, to deliver vehicles to customers in targeted volume and of high quality. Currently, Lotus Technology works with a BEV manufacturing facility in Wuhan, China, which is owned and operated by Geely Holding.

In the first nine months of 2024, Lotus delivered over 7,600 vehicles, marking a 136% increase compared to the same period in 2023, when 3,221 vehicles were delivered. The third quarter alone saw 2,744 vehicles delivered, a 54% increase over the 1,782 vehicles delivered in Q3 2023. Total revenue for the first nine months of 2024 amounted to $653 million, representing an impressive 105% year-on-year growth from the $318 million reported in the same period of 2023. In the third quarter of 2024, revenue was $255 million, showing a 36% increase from the $188 million in Q3 2023. The intelligent driving business showed remarkable growth, with revenue from external customers reaching $11 million, reflecting a year-on-year growth of 450%. Despite this, the gross profit margins declined, with Q3 2024 margins at 3%, down from 15% in Q3 2023. For the first nine months of 2024, the gross profit margin was 9%, compared to 11% in the same period of 2023.

How was Lotus Technology's performance in the first half of the year? Total revenue for the first half of 2024 was $398 million, representing a year-on-year increase of 206%. Lotus Technology's Average Selling Price (ASP) remained above $100,000, achieving growth far exceeding the industry and luxury car segment. Lotus Technology's gross margin for the first half of this year was 13%, compared to 5% in the same period of 2023.

In the first half of 2024, Lotus Technology achieved total product deliveries of 4,873 units, a 239% year-on-year increase, making it the fastest-growing brand among traditional luxury car brands. Global deliveries were balanced, with the four major markets (Europe, China, North America, and Rest of World) each contributing 20-30% of deliveries.

In the first half of 2024, as Lotus Technology began collaborating with several leading automotive brands by providing intelligent driving solutions and technology development services, technology-related revenue started to expand. With the continued increase in cumulative deliveries of lifestyle vehicles equipped with advanced hardware, the potential for generating paid software subscription revenue continues to grow.

Lotus Technology launched the Colin Chapman Bespoke Service, offering personalized options, designer customization, limited editions, and exclusive customization to meet the growing demands of its customers. The delivery of customized models is expected to further enhance the brand's luxury positioning and benefit Lotus Technology's long-term profit margins.

Deliveries of lifestyle vehicles saw a remarkable 126% year-on-year increase in the first nine months of 2024, constituting 52% of the total deliveries. Europe, being the home of the Lotus brand, demonstrated significant growth with a 372% year-on-year increase in vehicle deliveries during this period. The Rest of the World (ROW) region achieved a 110% year-on-year increase, as Lotus continued to expand into new markets. Furthermore, the company has surpassed 200 stores worldwide, with per-store sales steadily improving through operational optimization. The delivery breakdown for the first nine months of 2024 was 52% lifestyle vehicles and 48% sportscars. By region, deliveries were distributed as follows: Europe accounted for 35%, China for 25%, North America for 18%, and ROW for 22%. Regarding the store distribution, Europe held 34%, China had 25%, North America had 24%, and ROW had 17%.

In the first nine months of 2024, Lotus delivered 7,617 vehicles, which marks a 136% increase from the 3,221 units delivered during the same period in 2023. Revenue reached $653 million, representing a 105% year-on-year increase compared to $318 million in 2023. The cost of revenues also rose significantly by 109%, totaling $594 million compared to $284 million the previous year. As a result, gross profit was $59 million, up 75% from $34 million in 2023. However, the gross profit margin decreased to 9% from 11% in the previous year. For the third quarter of 2024, 2,744 vehicles were delivered, a 54% increase from 1,782 in the same quarter of 2023. Revenue in Q3 2024 was $255 million, a 36% increase over $188 million in Q3 2023. The cost of revenues for this quarter was $247 million, up 54% compared to $160 million in Q3 2023.

(“Lotus” or the “Company”), a leading global intelligent and luxury mobility provider, today announced its vehicle delivery results for the first ten months of 2024. Lotus Technology Inc. continues to achieve top-rate growth among traditional luxury car brands, delivering 8,631 vehicles in the first ten months of 2024. Europe and China continued to be the major markets for Lotus Technology, with each region contributing about 35% and 25% of the total deliveries, respectively. In October, Lotus Technology Inc. started an open beta test for its urban NOA (Navigate on Autopilot) in key cities. Certain over-the-air updates of intelligent driving, including highway assistant driving functions, were launched in Europe. As previously announced, Lotus Robotics, Lotus Technology's intelligent driving arm, provides intelligent driving solutions with global coverage to both Lotus-brand intelligent vehicles and other global leading auto conglomerates such as Lynk&Co, Farizon Auto, and a top Japanese Tier-one supplier. In October, Lotus Technology Inc. was awarded the Highly Commends honor at the Reuters Sustainability Awards 2024 in the Business Transformation category as the only awardee from the automotive industry. Lotus continues to drive technological innovation to meet market demand.

Global deliveries were balanced, with the four major markets (Europe, China, North America, and Rest of World) each contributing 20-30% of deliveries.

In the first half of 2024, as Lotus Technology began collaborating with several leading automotive brands by providing intelligent driving solutions and technology development services, technology-related revenue started to expand. With the continued increase in cumulative deliveries of lifestyle vehicles equipped with advanced hardware, the potential for generating paid software subscription revenue continues to grow.

Lotus Technology launched the Colin Chapman Bespoke Service, offering personalized options, designer customization, limited editions, and exclusive customization to meet the growing demands of its customers. The delivery of customized models is expected to further enhance the brand's luxury positioning and benefit Lotus Technology's long-term profit margins.

question: 路特斯科技是否拥有路特斯品牌？
rewritten: Does Lotus Tech own the Lotus brand?
answer:
Lotus Tech 通过与 Group Lotus Limited 签订的商标许可协议，获得了“Lotus”商标的使用权，主要用于与生活方式车辆相关的业务（不
包括跑车）。根据协议，Lotus Tech 发行了 47,995,443 股普通股作为商标许可的对价。此外，Lotus Tech 通过其全资子公司和可变利益实体
（VIE）及其子公司，主要负责设计、开发和销售豪华电池电动汽车（BEV生活方式车型）和豪华跑车。因此，Lotus Tech 在很大程度上控制了 Lotus 品牌
的使用和推广。以上信息基于2024年9月19日之前的知识。
content: On November 4, 2021, Lotus Technology Inc. entered into trademark license agreements with a related party (“Trademark License Agreements”), Group Lotus Limited, a wholly owned subsidiary of LGIL, which is ultimately controlled by the Controlling Shareholder of Lotus Technology Inc. Pursuant to the Trademark License Agreements, Lotus Technology Inc. received the “Lotus” trademark licenses for as long as Lotus Technology Inc. conducts business in relation to lifestyle vehicles (excluding sports cars): i) exclusively on lifestyle vehicles and parts and components related to those lifestyle vehicles that Lotus Technology Inc. designs, develops, manufactures, assembles, distributes, and sells or has designed, developed, manufactured, or assembled by any third party; ii) non-exclusively for Lotus Technology Inc.’s business of providing related after-sale services for the lifestyle vehicles; iii) non-exclusively on related products (excluding anything relating to sports cars) such as accessories that Lotus Technology Inc. designs, produces, distributes, and sells or has designed or produced by any third party on its behalf; iv) any other occasions in relation to the business conducted by Lotus Technology Inc. as duly approved by Lotus Technology Inc.’s Board (excluding anything relating to sports cars).

On November 4, 2021, Group Lotus Limited, a wholly owned subsidiary of LGIL, which is ultimately controlled by the Controlling Shareholder of the Company, entered into trademark license agreements with a related party (“Trademark License Agreements”). Pursuant to the Trademark License Agreements, the Group received the “Lotus” trademark licenses for as long as the Group conducts business related to lifestyle vehicles (excluding sports cars): i) exclusively for lifestyle vehicles and parts and components related to those vehicles that the Group designs, develops, manufactures, assembles, distributes, and sells, or has designed, developed, manufactured, or assembled by any third party; ii) non-exclusively for the Group’s business of providing related after-sale services for lifestyle vehicles; iii) non-exclusively for related products (excluding anything relating to sports cars), such as accessories that the Group designs, produces, distributes, and sells, or has designed or produced by any third party on its behalf; iv) any other occasions related to the business conducted by the Group as duly approved by the Group’s Board (excluding anything relating to sports cars). The Group intends to market and distribute its products under the “Lotus” brand indefinitely, and the trademark licenses are expected to contribute to cash flows indefinitely.

The equity interests of Lotus Tech UK and Lotus GmbH were transferred to Lotus Technology Corporation on December 29, 2021, and June 24, 2022, respectively. On November 4, 2021, Lotus Technology Corporation entered into trademark license agreements with a related party, Group Lotus Limited, a wholly-owned subsidiary of Lotus Group International Limited, or LGIL, pursuant to which Lotus Technology Corporation received the “Lotus” trademark licenses as long as it conducts business related to lifestyle vehicles (excluding sports cars). Lotus Technology Corporation issued 216,700,000 ordinary shares as consideration for such trademark. The above reorganization was completed on June 24, 2022. On November 12, 2021, the former Variable Interest Entity (VIE) and a third party established Ningbo Robotics Co., Ltd., or Ningbo Robotics, in which the former VIE held a 60% equity interest. In March 2022, the former VIE transferred its 60% legal equity interest in Ningbo Robotics to its then wholly-owned subsidiary, Sanya Lotus Venture Capital Co., Ltd. On March 15, 2022, Lotus Technology Corporation (LTC) declared a 10-for-1 stock split in the form of a stock dividend, which was distributed to all the shareholders of LTC in proportion to their respective shareholdings in LTC.

Geely Holding, a global mobility technology group with a proven track record in seeding BEV brands, has successfully incubated and revitalized a series of world-renowned brands with attractive financial profiles, including Volvo, Polestar, LYNK&CO., and Zeekr. Positioned as the only Geely Holding-affiliated brand with sports car DNA, Lotus Technology has received comprehensive support from Geely Holding in manufacturing, supply chain, research and development, logistics infrastructure, and human capital, and is in the process of transforming from a British sports car company to a global pioneer of high-performance electric vehicles to bridge the gap between traditional sports cars and a new generation of electric vehicles. The global luxury battery electric vehicle (BEV) market is currently underserved, with only approximately 10 existing luxury BEV models, compared to over 100 internal combustion engine (ICE) luxury models, leaving consumers with limited choices. As an early mover in the global luxury BEV market, Lotus Technology is leading the electrification transformation of this fast-growing luxury car segment, launching its E-segment BEV model years ahead of competitors. Lotus Technology launched its first fully electric Hyper-SUV, the Eletre, in 2022.

Lotus Technology believes that its R&D capability is one of its key competitive strengths. Drawn from Lotus brand sports car design heritage, deep automotive expertise, and next-generation technologies, Lotus Technology's proprietary 800-volt EPA is a high-performance platform for luxury electric vehicles, which was developed over five years of R&D efforts. The platform features supercharging capabilities, high energy conservation, and high-speed data transmission, with high adaptability that can accommodate varying battery sizes, motors, and component layouts across vehicle classes. Such superior design enables Lotus Technology to quickly roll out new models and ramp up production with competitive performance attributes and achieve economies of scale. Aside from the EPA, Lotus Technology has developed a leading ADAS with fully embedded L4-ready hardware capabilities enabled by the world’s first deployable LiDAR system and proprietary software system. Lotus Technology's wholly-owned R&D facilities across Europe and China demonstrate a seamless collaboration among highly experienced and dedicated Lotus teams to support its world-class R&D capabilities. Lotus Technology manufactures all battery electric vehicle (BEV) lifestyle models through a contract manufacturing partnership with Geely Holding, utilizing Geely Holding’s newly constructed, state-of-the-art manufacturing facilities dedicated to electric vehicles in Wuhan, China.

question: 路特斯科技的盈利水平和现金流情况怎么样？
rewritten: What is the profit level and cash flow situation of Lotus Tech?
answer:
Lotus Technology 自成立以来尚未实现盈利。根据最新的财务数据，截至2024年6月30日，公司净亏损为4.603亿美元，2023年、2022年和2
021年的净亏损分别为7.503亿美元、7.246亿美元和1.105亿美元。此外，公司在2024年上半年的经营活动产生的净现金流出为4.684亿美元，2023年
、2022年和2021年的净现金流出分别为3.869亿美元、3.514亿美元和1.265亿美元。截至2024年6月30日，公司的累计赤字为20亿美元，而2023
年底、2022年底和2021年底的累计赤字分别为16亿美元、8.468亿美元和1.228亿美元。尽管目前的财务状况较为紧张，但Lotus Technology 
通过 Win26 计划，计划在2026年实现正现金流和盈利。公司还设定了每年交付12,000辆汽车的目标，年增长率超过70%。以上信息基于2024年10月16日
之前的知识。
content: Lotus Technology has not been profitable since its inception. The company incurred a net loss of US\$460.3 million, US\$750.3 million, US\$724.6 million, and US\$110.5 million for the six months ended June 30, 2024, and in 2023, 2022, and 2021, respectively. In addition, Lotus Technology had negative net cash flows from operating activities of US\$468.4 million, US\$386.9 million, US\$351.4 million, and US\$126.5 million for the six months ended June 30, 2024, and in 2023, 2022, and 2021, respectively. As of June 30, 2024, and as of December 31, 2023, 2022, and 2021, the company's accumulated deficit was US\$2.0 billion, US\$1.6 billion, US\$846.8 million, and US\$122.8 million, respectively. Lotus Technology incurred capital expenditures of US\$49.5 million, US\$213.7 million, US\$133.3 million, and US\$34.6 million for the six months ended June 30, 2024, and in 2023, 2022, and 2021, respectively. Historically, Lotus Technology relied principally on proceeds from the issuance of exchangeable notes, convertible notes, and related party borrowings to finance its operations and business expansion. The company will require additional liquidity to continue its operations over the next 12 months. Lotus Technology is evaluating strategies to obtain the required additional funding for future operations.

If Lotus Technology does not effectively manage its cash and other liquid financial assets, execute its plan to increase profitability, and obtain additional financing, it may not be able to continue as a going concern.
